  properties     { title: string; locale: string; timeZone: string }
  sheets         Array<{ properties: { sheetId: number; title: string; index: number } }>

--- useGoogleSheetValues ---

Read cell values from a range in a spreadsheet. Values are returned as a 2D string array.

  import { useGoogleSheetValues } from '@reactjit/apis';

  const result: APIResult<GoogleSheetValues> = useGoogleSheetValues(
    token: string | null,
    spreadsheetId: string | null,
    range: string | null,
  );

Parameters:
  token          string | null  OAuth2 Bearer token
  spreadsheetId  string | null  Spreadsheet ID
  range          string | null  A1 notation range (e.g. 'Sheet1!A1:D20' or 'A:C')

Returns: APIResult<GoogleSheetValues>

GoogleSheetValues fields:
  range           string      Canonical range returned by the API
  majorDimension  string      'ROWS' (values[i] is row i) or 'COLUMNS'
  values          string[][]  2D array of cell values as strings

--- useGoogleSheetMutation ---

Get imperative operations for appending and updating spreadsheet data.

  import { useGoogleSheetMutation } from '@reactjit/apis';

  const mutation = useGoogleSheetMutation(token: string | null);

Parameters:
  token  string | null  OAuth2 Bearer token

Returns: object
  append   (spreadsheetId: string, range: string, values: string[][]) => Promise<any>
           Append rows after the last row of data in the given range.
           values is a 2D array where each inner array is a row of cell values.
           Uses USER_ENTERED input mode (formulas and dates are parsed).
  update   (spreadsheetId: string, range: string, values: string[][]) => Promise<any>
           Overwrite the specified range with new values. The range must be large enough
           to hold all provided values. Uses USER_ENTERED input mode.
  loading  boolean        True while a mutation is in flight
  error    Error | null   Last error, or null
seeAlso: useTodoistTasks — task management via Todoist REST API
useNotionDatabase — structured data in Notion databases

metadata.title: Home Assistant API Hooks
metadata.description: Hooks for the Home Assistant REST API — entity states, service calls, config, history, and logbook.

sections.overview:
The Home Assistant hooks wrap the Home Assistant local REST API. Authentication uses a long-lived access token (Bearer) — generate one in Settings > Profile > Long-Lived Access Tokens. You also need the base URL of your Home Assistant instance (e.g. http://homeassistant.local:8123).

Both baseUrl and token are required by every hook. Passing null for either skips the request. The polling hooks (useHAStates, useHAEntity) accept an optional interval parameter so entity state stays live on screen.

useHACallService is a mutation hook — it returns a call() function rather than reactive data. Use it to trigger automations, toggle lights, set thermostat values, and any other service action.
sections.api:
--- useHAStates ---

Fetch all entity states from Home Assistant. Optionally polls on an interval.

  import { useHAStates } from '@reactjit/apis/homeassistant';

  const result: APIResult<HAState[]> = useHAStates(baseUrl, token, opts?);

Parameters:
  baseUrl   string | null   Home Assistant base URL, e.g. 'http://homeassistant.local:8123'
  token     string | null   Long-lived access token.
  opts      object (optional)
    interval  number   Polling interval in ms. No polling if omitted.

Returns: APIResult<HAState[]>

HAState fields:
  entity_id     string   e.g. 'light.living_room', 'sensor.temperature'
  state         string   Entity state value, e.g. 'on', '21.5', 'unavailable'
  attributes    Record<string, any>   Entity-specific attributes
  last_changed  string   ISO 8601 timestamp
  last_updated  string   ISO 8601 timestamp

--- useHAEntity ---

Fetch a single entity's state by entity ID. Optionally polls on an interval.

  import { useHAEntity } from '@reactjit/apis/homeassistant';

  const result: APIResult<HAState> = useHAEntity(baseUrl, token, entityId, opts?);

Parameters:
  baseUrl    string | null
  token      string | null
  entityId   string | null   Entity ID to fetch, e.g. 'sensor.bedroom_temperature'. Pass null to skip.
  opts       object (optional)
    interval  number   Polling interval in ms.

Returns: APIResult<HAState>   Same HAState shape as above.

--- useHAConfig ---

Fetch the Home Assistant instance configuration (location, timezone, version, unit system).

  import { useHAConfig } from '@reactjit/apis/homeassistant';

  const result: APIResult<HAConfig> = useHAConfig(baseUrl, token);

Parameters:
  baseUrl   string | null
  token     string | null

Returns: APIResult<HAConfig>

HAConfig fields:
  location_name  string
  latitude       number
  longitude      number
  elevation      number
  unit_system    Record<string, string>   e.g. { temperature: '°C', length: 'km' }
  time_zone      string   IANA timezone string
  version        string   Home Assistant version

--- useHAServices ---

Fetch the list of all available service domains and their service definitions.

  import { useHAServices } from '@reactjit/apis/homeassistant';

  const result: APIResult<HAService[]> = useHAServices(baseUrl, token);

Parameters:
  baseUrl   string | null
  token     string | null

Returns: APIResult<HAService[]>

HAService fields:
  domain    string   e.g. 'light', 'switch', 'climate'
  services  Record<string, { description: string; fields: Record<string, any> }>

--- useHAHistory ---

Fetch the state history for a single entity over a recent time window.

  import { useHAHistory } from '@reactjit/apis/homeassistant';

  const result: APIResult<HAState[][]> = useHAHistory(baseUrl, token, entityId, opts?);

Parameters:
  baseUrl    string | null
  token      string | null
  entityId   string | null   Pass null to skip.
  opts       object (optional)
    hours   number   How many hours back to fetch. Default: 24.

Returns: APIResult<HAState[][]>   Outer array has one entry per entity matched (usually one). Inner arrays are time-ordered HAState snapshots.

--- useHALogbook ---

Fetch the logbook (human-readable event log) for an optional entity filter.

  import { useHALogbook } from '@reactjit/apis/homeassistant';

  const result: APIResult<HALogEntry[]> = useHALogbook(baseUrl, token, opts?);

Parameters:
  baseUrl   string | null
  token     string | null
  opts      object (optional)
    entityId  string   Filter log to a single entity.
    hours     number   How many hours back to fetch. Default: 24.

Returns: APIResult<HALogEntry[]>

HALogEntry fields:
  name       string
  message    string
  entity_id  string (optional)
  when       string   ISO 8601 timestamp

--- useHACallService ---

Imperative hook for calling Home Assistant services (toggle lights, set climate, trigger automations, etc.).

  import { useHACallService } from '@reactjit/apis/homeassistant';

  const ha = useHACallService(baseUrl, token);

Parameters:
  baseUrl   string | null
  token     string | null

Returns:
  call     (domain: string, service: string, data?: Record<string, any>) => Promise<any>
  loading  boolean   True while the service call is in flight.
  error    Error | null
seeAlso: weather — OpenWeatherMap hooks for outdoor weather data

metadata.title: Philips Hue
metadata.description: Hooks for reading and controlling Philips Hue lights, groups, scenes, and sensors from your local bridge.

sections.overview:
The Hue hooks talk directly to the local Hue bridge over HTTP — no cloud required. All reads use the reactive `useAPI` base hook (polling, loading/error state, manual refetch). Writes go through `useHueControl`, which wraps `useAPIMutation` and exposes three imperative methods: set a single light state, set an entire group action, or activate a named scene.

Auth: Press the physical link button on your bridge, then POST to `http://<bridgeIp>/api` with `{"devicetype":"myapp"}`. The bridge returns a username string — use that as your `apiKey` in every call below.
sections.api:
--- useHueLights ---

Fetch all lights registered on the bridge. Returns a dictionary keyed by light ID string.

  import { useHueLights } from '@reactjit/apis/hue';

  const result: APIResult<Record<string, HueLight>> = useHueLights(
    bridgeIp: string | null,
    apiKey:   string | null,
    opts?:    { interval?: number },
  );

Parameters:
  bridgeIp         string | null   Local IP of the Hue bridge (e.g. '192.168.1.10'). Pass null to disable.
  apiKey           string | null   API key (username) obtained from the bridge. Pass null to disable.
  opts.interval    number          Polling interval in milliseconds. Omit to fetch once.

Returns: APIResult<Record<string, HueLight>>
  data     Record<string, HueLight> | null   Map of light ID → HueLight
  loading  boolean
  error    Error | null
  refetch  () => void

HueLight fields:
  name              string
  type              string
  modelid           string
  manufacturername  string
  productname       string
  uniqueid          string
  state.on          boolean
  state.bri         number                       Brightness 0–254
  state.hue         number                       Hue 0–65535
  state.sat         number                       Saturation 0–254
  state.ct          number                       Color temperature in mireds
  state.xy          [number, number]             CIE xy color space coordinates
  state.colormode   'hs' | 'xy' | 'ct'
  state.reachable   boolean
  state.alert       string
  state.effect      string

--- useHueLight ---

Fetch a single light by its ID.

  import { useHueLight } from '@reactjit/apis/hue';

  const result: APIResult<HueLight> = useHueLight(
    bridgeIp: string | null,
    apiKey:   string | null,
    lightId:  string | null,
    opts?:    { interval?: number },
  );

Parameters:
  bridgeIp         string | null   Local bridge IP. Pass null to disable.
  apiKey           string | null   Bridge API key. Pass null to disable.
  lightId          string | null   Light ID as a string (e.g. '3'). Pass null to disable.
  opts.interval    number          Polling interval in milliseconds.

Returns: APIResult<HueLight>
  (same fields as described under useHueLights)

--- useHueGroups ---

Fetch all groups (rooms and zones) on the bridge.

  import { useHueGroups } from '@reactjit/apis/hue';

  const result: APIResult<Record<string, HueGroup>> = useHueGroups(
    bridgeIp: string | null,
    apiKey:   string | null,
  );

Parameters:
  bridgeIp  string | null
  apiKey    string | null

Returns: APIResult<Record<string, HueGroup>>
  data  Record<string, HueGroup> | null   Map of group ID → HueGroup

HueGroup fields:
  name           string
  lights         string[]               Light IDs belonging to this group
  type           string
  class          string | undefined      Room class (e.g. 'Living room', 'Bedroom')
  state.all_on   boolean                True if every light in the group is on
  state.any_on   boolean                True if at least one light is on
  action         HueLight['state']      The last action applied to the group

--- useHueSensors ---

Fetch all sensors registered on the bridge (motion sensors, switches, temperature probes, etc.).

  import { useHueSensors } from '@reactjit/apis/hue';

  const result: APIResult<Record<string, HueSensor>> = useHueSensors(
    bridgeIp: string | null,
    apiKey:   string | null,
  );

Parameters:
  bridgeIp  string | null
  apiKey    string | null

Returns: APIResult<Record<string, HueSensor>>
  data  Record<string, HueSensor> | null   Map of sensor ID → HueSensor

HueSensor fields:
  name      string
  type      string
  modelid   string
  uniqueid  string
  state     Record<string, any>   Sensor-specific state (e.g. { presence: true } for motion)
  config    Record<string, any>   Sensor configuration

--- useHueScenes ---

Fetch all saved scenes on the bridge.

  import { useHueScenes } from '@reactjit/apis/hue';

  const result: APIResult<Record<string, HueScene>> = useHueScenes(
    bridgeIp: string | null,
    apiKey:   string | null,
  );

Parameters:
  bridgeIp  string | null
  apiKey    string | null

Returns: APIResult<Record<string, HueScene>>
  data  Record<string, HueScene> | null   Map of scene ID → HueScene

HueScene fields:
  name         string
  type         string
  group        string | undefined   Group ID this scene belongs to
  lights       string[]             Light IDs included in the scene
  lightstates  Record<string, Partial<HueLight['state']>> | undefined

--- useHueControl ---

Imperative mutation hook for sending commands to the bridge. Returns methods for setting individual light states, group actions, and activating scenes.

  import { useHueControl } from '@reactjit/apis/hue';

  const ctrl = useHueControl(
    bridgeIp: string | null,
    apiKey:   string | null,
  );

Parameters:
  bridgeIp  string | null
  apiKey    string | null

Returns: object
  setLight      (lightId: string, state: Partial<HueLight['state']>) => Promise<any>
                  Send a partial state update to a single light (e.g. { on: true, bri: 200 })
  setGroup      (groupId: string, action: Partial<HueLight['state']>) => Promise<any>
                  Apply an action to every light in a group simultaneously
  activateScene (sceneId: string, groupId?: string) => Promise<any>
                  Activate a saved scene. groupId defaults to '0' (all groups)
  loading       boolean   True while any mutation request is in flight
  error         Error | null

--- hueXYToHex (utility) ---

Convert a Hue light's CIE xy coordinates and brightness to an approximate sRGB hex string. Useful for rendering a color swatch from live light state.

  import { hueXYToHex } from '@reactjit/apis/hue';

  const hex: string = hueXYToHex(x: number, y: number, bri: number);

Parameters:
  x    number   CIE x coordinate (from state.xy[0])
  y    number   CIE y coordinate (from state.xy[1])
  bri  number   Brightness 0–254 (from state.bri)

Returns: string   Hex color string, e.g. '#f5a623'
seeAlso: nasa — another local-network-friendly hook, useful for offline-first HUD layouts
useAPIMutation — low-level imperative mutation hook used internally by useHueControl

metadata.title: API Reference
metadata.description: Hub page for complete API reference documentation

sections.overview:
This section contains the complete API reference for ReactJIT. The style properties document lists every property available in the Style interface with types, valid values, and descriptions. The types document provides the full TypeScript type definitions for all components, events, and animation interfaces.

These references are derived directly from the source code in packages/shared/src/types.ts and related files.

examples.title: Section Contents
examples.code:
12-api-reference/
  style-properties.txt - Every Style property with type, values, description
  types.txt            - All TypeScript type definitions

metadata.title: Last.fm API Hooks
metadata.description: Hooks for the Last.fm API — now playing, recent tracks, top artists/tracks/albums, and user info.

sections.overview:
The Last.fm hooks wrap the Last.fm REST API (ws.audioscrobbler.com/2.0). Authentication uses an API key passed as a query parameter — get one at https://www.last.fm/api/account/create. No OAuth is required for read-only user data.

All hooks take the API key and a username as the first two arguments. Passing null for either skips the request. Hooks that display live "now playing" state accept an interval option and poll on a timer.

Last.fm's JSON responses use unconventional field names (artist fields may be objects or plain strings depending on context, images use '#text' as the URL key). The types reflect the actual API response shape. The package also exports a lastfmImage() utility to extract the best available image URL from an image array.
sections.api:
--- useLastFMRecentTracks ---

Fetch a user's recently scrobbled tracks. The first item may be a currently-playing track (check for '@attr'.nowplaying).

  import { useLastFMRecentTracks } from '@reactjit/apis/lastfm';

  const result: APIResult<{ recenttracks: { track: LastFMTrack[] } }> =
    useLastFMRecentTracks(apiKey, user, opts?);

Parameters:
  apiKey   string | null
  user     string | null   Last.fm username. Pass null to skip.
  opts     object (optional)
    limit     number   Max tracks to return. Default: 20.
    interval  number   Polling interval in ms. No polling if omitted.

Returns: APIResult<{ recenttracks: { track: LastFMTrack[] } }>
  data.recenttracks.track   LastFMTrack[]

--- useLastFMNowPlaying ---

Poll for the track a user is currently scrobbling. Returns the same response shape as useLastFMRecentTracks (limit 1). The now-playing track has '@attr'.nowplaying === 'true'; a recently-played track has a date field instead.

  import { useLastFMNowPlaying } from '@reactjit/apis/lastfm';

  const result: APIResult<{ recenttracks: { track: LastFMTrack[] } }> =
    useLastFMNowPlaying(apiKey, user, opts?);

Parameters:
  apiKey   string | null
  user     string | null
  opts     object (optional)
    interval  number   Polling interval in ms. Default: 10000.

Returns: APIResult<{ recenttracks: { track: LastFMTrack[] } }>

--- useLastFMTopArtists ---

Fetch a user's top artists over a time period.

  import { useLastFMTopArtists } from '@reactjit/apis/lastfm';

  const result: APIResult<{ topartists: { artist: LastFMArtist[] } }> =
    useLastFMTopArtists(apiKey, user, opts?);

Parameters:
  apiKey   string | null
  user     string | null
  opts     object (optional)
    period  'overall' | '7day' | '1month' | '3month' | '6month' | '12month'   Default: none (API default: overall)
    limit   number   Default: 10.

Returns: APIResult<{ topartists: { artist: LastFMArtist[] } }>
  data.topartists.artist   LastFMArtist[]   Ordered by play count descending.

--- useLastFMTopTracks ---

Fetch a user's top tracks over a time period.

  import { useLastFMTopTracks } from '@reactjit/apis/lastfm';

  const result: APIResult<{ toptracks: { track: LastFMTrack[] } }> =
    useLastFMTopTracks(apiKey, user, opts?);

Parameters:
  apiKey   string | null
  user     string | null
  opts     object (optional)
    period  'overall' | '7day' | '1month' | '3month' | '6month' | '12month'
    limit   number   Default: 10.

Returns: APIResult<{ toptracks: { track: LastFMTrack[] } }>

--- useLastFMTopAlbums ---

Fetch a user's top albums over a time period.

  import { useLastFMTopAlbums } from '@reactjit/apis/lastfm';

  const result: APIResult<{ topalbums: { album: LastFMAlbum[] } }> =
    useLastFMTopAlbums(apiKey, user, opts?);

Parameters:
  apiKey   string | null
  user     string | null
  opts     object (optional)
    period  'overall' | '7day' | '1month' | '3month' | '6month' | '12month'
    limit   number   Default: 10.

Returns: APIResult<{ topalbums: { album: LastFMAlbum[] } }>

--- useLastFMUser ---

Fetch a user's profile information.

  import { useLastFMUser } from '@reactjit/apis/lastfm';

  const result: APIResult<{ user: LastFMUser }> = useLastFMUser(apiKey, user);

Parameters:
  apiKey   string | null
  user     string | null

Returns: APIResult<{ user: LastFMUser }>

LastFMUser fields:
  name        string
  realname    string
  image       LastFMImage[]
  playcount   string   Total scrobble count (returned as a string by the API)
  registered  { unixtime: string }
  url         string
  country     string

--- lastfmImage ---

Extract the best available image URL from a Last.fm image array. Last.fm returns images as an array of { '#text': url; size: ... } objects. This utility picks the preferred size and falls back to the last available entry.

  import { lastfmImage } from '@reactjit/apis/lastfm';

  const url: string | null = lastfmImage(images, preferSize?);

Parameters:
  images      LastFMImage[] | undefined
  preferSize  string   Image size to prefer. Default: 'extralarge'. Options: 'small', 'medium', 'large', 'extralarge', 'mega'.

Returns: string | null — null if no images or all URLs are empty.
seeAlso: spotify — Spotify Web API hooks with playback control (requires OAuth token)

metadata.title: NASA Open APIs
metadata.description: Hooks for Astronomy Picture of the Day, Mars rover photos, near-Earth object feeds, and EPIC Earth imagery.

sections.overview:
The NASA hooks wrap four public NASA endpoints: Astronomy Picture of the Day (APOD), Mars rover photo archives, Near-Earth Object (NEO) tracking, and EPIC satellite imagery of Earth. All hooks are read-only and built on the reactive `useAPI` base — they start fetching on mount, surface `loading`/`error`/`data`, and expose a manual `refetch`.

Auth: All endpoints accept `DEMO_KEY` for low-rate testing (30 requests/hour). Register a free API key at https://api.nasa.gov for higher limits (1000 requests/hour). Pass `null` as `apiKey` and the hooks fall back to `DEMO_KEY` automatically.
sections.api:
--- useNASAApod ---

Fetch the Astronomy Picture of the Day. Pass a specific date to get a historical image, or a count to get a random selection.

  import { useNASAApod } from '@reactjit/apis/nasa';

  const result: APIResult<NASAAPOD | NASAAPOD[]> = useNASAApod(
    apiKey: string | null,
    opts?:  { date?: string; count?: number },
  );

Parameters:
  apiKey      string | null   NASA API key, or null to use DEMO_KEY
  opts.date   string          ISO date string (e.g. '2024-07-04'). Returns a single NASAAPOD.
  opts.count  number          Return this many random APODs. Returns NASAAPOD[].

Returns: APIResult<NASAAPOD | NASAAPOD[]>
  data     NASAAPOD | NASAAPOD[] | null
  loading  boolean
  error    Error | null
  refetch  () => void

NASAAPOD fields:
  date           string               ISO date
  title          string
  explanation    string               Full text description
  url            string               Image or video URL
  hdurl          string | undefined   High-resolution image URL (images only)
  media_type     'image' | 'video'
  copyright      string | undefined
  thumbnail_url  string | undefined   Video thumbnail (videos only)

--- useNASAMarsPhotos ---

Fetch photos taken by a Mars rover on a given Martian sol (day) or Earth date. Supports all four rovers and optional camera filtering.

  import { useNASAMarsPhotos } from '@reactjit/apis/nasa';

  const result: APIResult<{ photos: NASAMarsPhoto[] }> = useNASAMarsPhotos(
    apiKey: string | null,
    opts?:  {
      rover?:     'curiosity' | 'opportunity' | 'spirit' | 'perseverance';
      sol?:       number;
      earthDate?: string;
      camera?:    string;
    },
  );

Parameters:
  apiKey          string | null   NASA API key, or null for DEMO_KEY
  opts.rover      string          Which rover to query. Defaults to 'curiosity'.
  opts.sol        number          Martian sol number. Defaults to 1000 when no earthDate is given.
  opts.earthDate  string          ISO date (e.g. '2023-01-15'). Takes precedence over sol.
  opts.camera     string          Filter by camera abbreviation (e.g. 'NAVCAM', 'FHAZ', 'MAST').

Returns: APIResult<{ photos: NASAMarsPhoto[] }>
  data.photos  NASAMarsPhoto[]

NASAMarsPhoto fields:
  id          number
  sol         number
  img_src     string               Direct image URL
  earth_date  string
  camera      { id, name, full_name }
  rover       { id, name, status, landing_date, launch_date }

--- useNASANeoFeed ---

Fetch Near-Earth Object (asteroid) close-approach data for a date range. Returns a date-keyed dictionary of asteroid arrays plus a total element count.

  import { useNASANeoFeed } from '@reactjit/apis/nasa';

  const result: APIResult<NASANeoFeed> = useNASANeoFeed(
    apiKey: string | null,
    opts?:  { startDate?: string; endDate?: string },
  );

Parameters:
  apiKey           string | null   NASA API key, or null for DEMO_KEY
  opts.startDate   string          ISO start date. Defaults to today.
  opts.endDate     string          ISO end date. Maximum 7-day window enforced by NASA.

Returns: APIResult<NASANeoFeed>
  data.element_count          number
  data.near_earth_objects     Record<string, NASANeoObject[]>   Keyed by ISO date string

NASANeoObject fields:
  id                                  string
  name                                string
  nasa_jpl_url                        string
  absolute_magnitude_h                number
  is_potentially_hazardous_asteroid   boolean
  estimated_diameter.kilometers       { estimated_diameter_min, estimated_diameter_max }
  estimated_diameter.meters           { estimated_diameter_min, estimated_diameter_max }
  close_approach_data                 Array of:
    close_approach_date                 string
    relative_velocity.kilometers_per_hour   string
    miss_distance.kilometers               string
    miss_distance.lunar                    string

--- useNASAEPIC ---

Fetch EPIC (Earth Polychromatic Imaging Camera) imagery. EPIC is a camera aboard the DSCOVR spacecraft that images the full sunlit face of Earth roughly every two hours.

  import { useNASAEPIC } from '@reactjit/apis/nasa';

  const result: APIResult<NASAEPICImage[]> = useNASAEPIC(
    apiKey: string | null,
    opts?:  { collection?: 'natural' | 'enhanced'; date?: string },
  );

Parameters:
  apiKey             string | null              NASA API key, or null for DEMO_KEY
  opts.collection    'natural' | 'enhanced'     Color collection. Defaults to 'natural'.
  opts.date          string                     ISO date. Omit for the most recent available.

Returns: APIResult<NASAEPICImage[]>
  data  NASAEPICImage[]

NASAEPICImage fields:
  identifier   string
  caption      string
  image        string   Image filename (without extension) — pass to nasaEPICImageUrl()
  date         string   ISO datetime string
  coords.centroid_coordinates   { lat: number; lon: number }

--- nasaEPICImageUrl (utility) ---

Build a full PNG URL for an EPIC image object. The EPIC archive URL structure encodes the date into directory segments, so this helper does the transformation for you.

  import { nasaEPICImageUrl } from '@reactjit/apis/nasa';

  const url: string = nasaEPICImageUrl(
    image:       NASAEPICImage,
    collection?: 'natural' | 'enhanced',
  );

Parameters:
  image       NASAEPICImage   An item from the useNASAEPIC result array
  collection  string          Defaults to 'natural'

Returns: string   Full PNG URL, e.g. 'https://epic.gsfc.nasa.gov/archive/natural/2024/07/04/png/epic_1b_20240704120022.png'
seeAlso: coingecko — another zero-auth API with live polling support
useAPI — base hook used internally by all NASA hooks

metadata.title: Notion Hooks
metadata.description: Hooks for querying Notion databases, reading pages and blocks, and writing new pages from ReactJIT components.

sections.overview:
The Notion hooks in @reactjit/apis connect to the Notion REST API v1. You need an internal integration token — create one at https://www.notion.so/my-integrations and share it with the databases or pages you want to read.

Read hooks (useNotionPage, useNotionBlocks) use reactive polling via useAPI. The database query hooks (useNotionDatabases, useNotionDatabase) use the POST-based search and query endpoints internally, so they are implemented with useAPIMutation but behave like read hooks — they auto-fetch on mount and expose a refetch() method. Write operations go through useNotionMutation, which returns imperative functions.

The Notion API version pinned by these hooks is 2022-06-28.
sections.api:
--- useNotionDatabases ---

Search for all databases the integration has been granted access to.

  import { useNotionDatabases } from '@reactjit/apis';

  const result: APIResult<NotionPaginated<NotionDatabase>> = useNotionDatabases(
    token: string | null,
  );

Parameters:
  token  string | null  Internal integration token

Returns: APIResult<NotionPaginated<NotionDatabase>>

NotionDatabase fields:
  id           string              UUID of the database
  title        NotionRichText[]    Database title as rich text
  description  NotionRichText[]
  url          string              notion.so URL
  properties   Record<string, { id: string; type: string; name: string }>
               Map of property key to schema definition

NotionPaginated<T> fields:
  results      T[]                 Page of results
  has_more     boolean             Whether more pages exist
  next_cursor  string | null       Cursor for next page, or null

--- useNotionDatabase ---

Query rows from a specific database with optional filter and sort. Uses Notion's database query endpoint (POST).

  import { useNotionDatabase } from '@reactjit/apis';

  const result: APIResult<NotionPaginated<NotionPage>> = useNotionDatabase(
    token: string | null,
    databaseId: string | null,
    opts?: { filter?: any; sorts?: any[] },
  );

Parameters:
  token       string | null   Integration token
  databaseId  string | null   UUID of the database to query
  opts        object | undefined
    filter    any             Notion filter object (see Notion API docs)
    sorts     any[]           Array of Notion sort objects

Returns: APIResult<NotionPaginated<NotionPage>>

NotionPage fields:
  id                string
  url               string
  created_time      string              ISO 8601
  last_edited_time  string              ISO 8601
  properties        Record<string, any>  Property values (shape varies by property type)
  icon              { type: string; emoji?: string } | null
  cover             { type: string; external?: { url: string } } | null

--- useNotionPage ---

Fetch a single Notion page by its ID.

  import { useNotionPage } from '@reactjit/apis';

  const result: APIResult<NotionPage> = useNotionPage(
    token: string | null,
    pageId: string | null,
  );

Parameters:
  token   string | null  Integration token
  pageId  string | null  UUID of the page

Returns: APIResult<NotionPage>

--- useNotionBlocks ---

Fetch the child blocks of a page or block. Returns the top-level block tree only — call recursively with a block's ID to fetch nested children.

  import { useNotionBlocks } from '@reactjit/apis';

  const result: APIResult<NotionPaginated<NotionBlock>> = useNotionBlocks(
    token: string | null,
    blockId: string | null,
  );

Parameters:
  token    string | null  Integration token
  blockId  string | null  UUID of the page or parent block

Returns: APIResult<NotionPaginated<NotionBlock>>

NotionBlock fields:
  id            string   UUID of the block
  type          string   'paragraph', 'heading_1', 'bulleted_list_item', 'to_do', 'image', etc.
  has_children  boolean  Whether this block has nested children
  [key: string] any      Type-specific content at the key matching the type field

--- useNotionMutation ---

Get imperative write operations: create pages and update existing page properties.

  import { useNotionMutation } from '@reactjit/apis';

  const mutation = useNotionMutation(token: string | null);

Parameters:
  token  string | null  Integration token

Returns: object
  createPage   (parentId: string, properties: Record<string, any>, children?: any[]) => Promise<any>
               Create a new page in a database. parentId is the database UUID.
               properties follows the Notion property value format.
               children is an optional array of block objects.
  updatePage   (pageId: string, properties: Record<string, any>) => Promise<any>
               Update properties of an existing page. Only provided keys are changed.
  loading      boolean        True while any mutation is in flight
  error        Error | null   Last mutation error, or null

--- Shared Types ---

NotionRichText fields:
  type        'text'
  text        { content: string; link?: { url: string } | null }
  plain_text  string  Convenience field — the text content without formatting
seeAlso: useTodoistTasks — task list management via Todoist REST API
useGoogleSheet — read and write Google Sheets data

metadata.title: Plex / Jellyfin / Emby Hooks
metadata.description: Hooks for reading libraries, browsing media, and monitoring active sessions from Plex, Jellyfin, and Emby servers.

sections.overview:
The @reactjit/apis package includes hooks for both Plex (token-based) and Jellyfin/Emby (API key-based) self-hosted media servers. All hooks are passive — they fetch on mount and can be polled automatically. Pass null for any credential argument to suppress fetching entirely, which makes it safe to render components before credentials are available.

Plex hooks use the X-Plex-Token header. Jellyfin and Emby hooks use the MediaBrowser Token authorization header. Both server families are covered by a single import.
sections.api:
--- usePlexLibraries ---

Fetch all library sections configured on the Plex server.

  import { usePlexLibraries } from '@reactjit/apis';

  const result: APIResult<{ MediaContainer: { Directory: PlexLibrary[] } }> = usePlexLibraries(
    baseUrl: string | null,
    token: string | null,
  );

Parameters:
  baseUrl  string | null  Base URL of the Plex server (e.g. 'http://localhost:32400')
  token    string | null  Plex authentication token

Returns: APIResult<{ MediaContainer: { Directory: PlexLibrary[] } }>

PlexLibrary fields:
  key      string  Section ID (used in usePlexLibrary)
  title    string  Human-readable library name
  type     string  'movie', 'show', 'music', or 'photo'
  agent    string  Metadata agent identifier
  scanner  string  Scanner identifier

--- usePlexLibrary ---

Fetch all items in a specific library section.

  import { usePlexLibrary } from '@reactjit/apis';

  const result: APIResult<{ MediaContainer: { Metadata: PlexMediaItem[] } }> = usePlexLibrary(
    baseUrl: string | null,
    token: string | null,
    sectionId: string | null,
  );

Parameters:
  baseUrl    string | null  Base URL of the Plex server
  token      string | null  Plex authentication token
  sectionId  string | null  Library section key from usePlexLibraries

Returns: APIResult<{ MediaContainer: { Metadata: PlexMediaItem[] } }>

PlexMediaItem fields:
  ratingKey      string   Unique item identifier
  title          string   Item title
  type           string   'movie', 'show', 'episode', etc.
  year           number | undefined
  thumb          string | undefined  Thumbnail path (append to baseUrl)
  art            string | undefined  Background art path
  summary        string | undefined
  duration       number | undefined  Duration in milliseconds
  addedAt        number   Unix timestamp
  updatedAt      number   Unix timestamp
  viewCount      number | undefined  Times played
  lastViewedAt   number | undefined  Unix timestamp of last play

--- usePlexRecentlyAdded ---

Fetch the most recently added items across all libraries.

  import { usePlexRecentlyAdded } from '@reactjit/apis';

  const result: APIResult<{ MediaContainer: { Metadata: PlexMediaItem[] } }> = usePlexRecentlyAdded(
    baseUrl: string | null,
    token: string | null,
    opts?: { limit?: number },
  );

Parameters:
  baseUrl  string | null   Base URL of the Plex server
  token    string | null   Plex authentication token
  opts     object | undefined
    limit  number          Number of items to return (default: 20)

Returns: APIResult<{ MediaContainer: { Metadata: PlexMediaItem[] } }>

--- usePlexSessions ---

Fetch currently active playback sessions. Supports automatic polling.

  import { usePlexSessions } from '@reactjit/apis';

  const result: APIResult<{ MediaContainer: { Metadata?: PlexSession[] } }> = usePlexSessions(
    baseUrl: string | null,
    token: string | null,
    opts?: { interval?: number },
  );

Parameters:
  baseUrl  string | null   Base URL of the Plex server
  token    string | null   Plex authentication token
  opts     object | undefined
    interval  number       Polling interval in milliseconds (default: 10000)

Returns: APIResult<{ MediaContainer: { Metadata?: PlexSession[] } }>
  Metadata is undefined when no sessions are active.

PlexSession fields:
  sessionKey   string   Unique session identifier
  title        string   Title of the playing item
  type         string   Media type
  thumb        string | undefined  Thumbnail path
  User         { title: string }
  Player       { title: string; state: string; platform: string }
               state is 'playing', 'paused', or 'buffering'
  viewOffset   number   Current position in milliseconds
  duration     number   Total duration in milliseconds

--- useJellyfinLibraries ---

Fetch all virtual library folders from a Jellyfin or Emby server.

  import { useJellyfinLibraries } from '@reactjit/apis';

  const result: APIResult<{ Items: JellyfinLibrary[] }> = useJellyfinLibraries(
    baseUrl: string | null,
    apiKey: string | null,
  );

Parameters:
  baseUrl  string | null  Base URL of the Jellyfin/Emby server
  apiKey   string | null  API key from the server dashboard

Returns: APIResult<{ Items: JellyfinLibrary[] }>

JellyfinLibrary fields:
  Id              string
  Name            string
  CollectionType  string   'movies', 'tvshows', 'music', etc.
  ImageTags       Record<string, string> | undefined

--- useJellyfinItems ---

Query media items with flexible filtering and sorting.

  import { useJellyfinItems } from '@reactjit/apis';

  const result: APIResult<{ Items: JellyfinItem[]; TotalRecordCount: number }> = useJellyfinItems(
    baseUrl: string | null,
    apiKey: string | null,
    opts?: { parentId?: string; type?: string; limit?: number; sortBy?: string },
  );

Parameters:
  baseUrl  string | null   Base URL of the Jellyfin/Emby server
  apiKey   string | null   API key
  opts     object | undefined
    parentId  string       Filter to a specific library or folder
    type      string       Item type filter (e.g. 'Movie', 'Series')
    limit     number       Max items to return (default: 20)
    sortBy    string       Sort field (default: 'DateCreated')

Returns: APIResult<{ Items: JellyfinItem[]; TotalRecordCount: number }>

JellyfinItem fields:
  Id               string
  Name             string
  Type             string
  Overview         string | undefined
  ProductionYear   number | undefined
  ImageTags        Record<string, string> | undefined
  RunTimeTicks     number | undefined  Duration in 100-nanosecond ticks
  CommunityRating  number | undefined
  DateCreated      string | undefined

--- useJellyfinSessions ---

Fetch currently active playback sessions from a Jellyfin/Emby server. Supports polling.

  import { useJellyfinSessions } from '@reactjit/apis';

  const result: APIResult<JellyfinSession[]> = useJellyfinSessions(
    baseUrl: string | null,
    apiKey: string | null,
    opts?: { interval?: number },
  );

Parameters:
  baseUrl  string | null   Base URL of the server
  apiKey   string | null   API key
  opts     object | undefined
    interval  number       Polling interval in milliseconds (default: 10000)

Returns: APIResult<JellyfinSession[]>

JellyfinSession fields:
  Id               string
  UserName         string
  Client           string
  DeviceName       string
  NowPlayingItem   JellyfinItem | undefined  Absent when idle
  PlayState        { PositionTicks: number; IsPaused: boolean } | undefined

--- useJellyfinLatest ---

Fetch the latest items a user has added or accessed. Requires a user ID.

  import { useJellyfinLatest } from '@reactjit/apis';

  const result: APIResult<JellyfinItem[]> = useJellyfinLatest(
    baseUrl: string | null,
    apiKey: string | null,
    userId: string | null,
    opts?: { limit?: number },
  );

Parameters:
  baseUrl  string | null   Base URL of the server
  apiKey   string | null   API key
  userId   string | null   Jellyfin user ID
  opts     object | undefined
    limit  number          Max items to return (default: 20)

Returns: APIResult<JellyfinItem[]>

--- jellyfinImage (utility) ---

Build a Jellyfin image URL. Not a hook — call it directly in JSX.

  import { jellyfinImage } from '@reactjit/apis';

  const url: string = jellyfinImage(
    baseUrl: string,
    itemId: string,
    tag: string,
    type?: string,
    maxWidth?: number,
  );

Parameters:
  baseUrl   string  Base URL of the server
  itemId    string  Item ID
  tag       string  Image tag from ImageTags
  type      string  Image type (default: 'Primary')
  maxWidth  number  Max image width for server-side resize (default: 400)

Returns: string — ready-to-use image URL for use in <Image src={...} />
seeAlso: useTraktHistory — watch history and watchlists from Trakt.tv
useSpotify — currently playing and library hooks for Spotify

metadata.title: Spotify API Hooks
metadata.description: Hooks for the Spotify Web API — now playing, top tracks/artists, playlists, search, and playback control.

sections.overview:
The Spotify hooks wrap the Spotify Web API. Authentication is handled via OAuth2 Bearer tokens — get one from the Spotify Developer Dashboard at https://developer.spotify.com. Pass the token as the first argument to every hook. Hooks that accept a null token skip the request entirely and return { data: null, loading: false, error: null }, so conditional rendering is safe without any extra guards.

All hooks return APIResult<T>, a stable object containing data, loading, error, and refetch. useSpotifyNowPlaying polls on a configurable interval (default 5 s). useSpotifyPlayback is a mutation hook — it returns action callbacks rather than reactive data.
sections.api:
--- useSpotifyNowPlaying ---

Poll the currently-playing track. Re-fetches on a timer so the display stays live.

  import { useSpotifyNowPlaying } from '@reactjit/apis/spotify';

  const result: APIResult<SpotifyNowPlaying> = useSpotifyNowPlaying(token, opts?);

Parameters:
  token   string | null   OAuth2 Bearer token. Pass null to skip the request.
  opts    object (optional)
    interval  number   Polling interval in ms. Default: 5000.

Returns: APIResult<SpotifyNowPlaying>
  data     SpotifyNowPlaying | null
  loading  boolean
  error    Error | null
  refetch  () => void

SpotifyNowPlaying fields:
  is_playing               boolean
  progress_ms              number
  item                     SpotifyTrack | null
  currently_playing_type   string

--- useSpotifyTopTracks ---

Fetch the authenticated user's top tracks over a time range.

  import { useSpotifyTopTracks } from '@reactjit/apis/spotify';

  const result: APIResult<SpotifyTopItem<SpotifyTrack>> = useSpotifyTopTracks(token, opts?);

Parameters:
  token   string | null
  opts    object (optional)
    timeRange  'short_term' | 'medium_term' | 'long_term'   Default: none (API default applies)
    limit      number                                         Max results. Default: none.

Returns: APIResult<SpotifyTopItem<SpotifyTrack>>
  data.items   SpotifyTrack[]
  data.total   number

--- useSpotifyTopArtists ---

Fetch the authenticated user's top artists over a time range.

  import { useSpotifyTopArtists } from '@reactjit/apis/spotify';

  const result: APIResult<SpotifyTopItem<SpotifyArtist>> = useSpotifyTopArtists(token, opts?);

Parameters:
  token   string | null
  opts    object (optional)
    timeRange  'short_term' | 'medium_term' | 'long_term'
    limit      number

Returns: APIResult<SpotifyTopItem<SpotifyArtist>>
  data.items   SpotifyArtist[]
  data.total   number

--- useSpotifyRecentTracks ---

Fetch the authenticated user's recently played tracks.

  import { useSpotifyRecentTracks } from '@reactjit/apis/spotify';

  const result: APIResult<{ items: RecentItem[] }> = useSpotifyRecentTracks(token, opts?);

Parameters:
  token   string | null
  opts    object (optional)
    limit  number   Max results. Default: 20.

Returns: APIResult<{ items: Array<{ track: SpotifyTrack; played_at: string }> }>

--- useSpotifyPlaylists ---

Fetch the authenticated user's saved playlists.

  import { useSpotifyPlaylists } from '@reactjit/apis/spotify';

  const result: APIResult<SpotifyPaginated<SpotifyPlaylist>> = useSpotifyPlaylists(token, opts?);

Parameters:
  token   string | null
  opts    object (optional)
    limit  number   Default: 50.

Returns: APIResult<SpotifyPaginated<SpotifyPlaylist>>
  data.items   SpotifyPlaylist[]
  data.total   number
  data.limit   number
  data.offset  number
  data.next    string | null

SpotifyPlaylist fields:
  id           string
  name         string
  description  string
  images       SpotifyImage[]
  tracks       { total: number }
  owner        { display_name: string }

--- useSpotifySearch ---

Search the Spotify catalog by query string.

  import { useSpotifySearch } from '@reactjit/apis/spotify';

  const result: APIResult<SearchResults> = useSpotifySearch(token, query, opts?);

Parameters:
  token   string | null
  query   string | null   Pass null to skip the request (e.g. while the user is typing).
  opts    object (optional)
    type   string   Comma-separated Spotify object types. Default: 'track,artist'.
    limit  number   Default: 20.

Returns: APIResult<{ tracks?: SpotifyPaginated<SpotifyTrack>; artists?: SpotifyPaginated<SpotifyArtist> }>

--- useSpotifyPlayback ---

Imperative playback control. Returns action callbacks rather than reactive data.

  import { useSpotifyPlayback } from '@reactjit/apis/spotify';

  const controls = useSpotifyPlayback(token);

Parameters:
  token   string | null

Returns:
  play      () => Promise<any>   Resume playback
  pause     () => Promise<any>   Pause playback
  next      () => Promise<any>   Skip to next track
  previous  () => Promise<any>   Skip to previous track
  loading   boolean              True while a control command is in flight
  error     Error | null
seeAlso: lastfm — scrobble history, top charts, and now playing via Last.fm (no OAuth required)

metadata.title: Steam Web API
metadata.description: Hooks for fetching Steam player profiles, owned and recently played games, friend lists, achievements, and store app details.

sections.overview:
The Steam hooks wrap the official Steam Web API and the Steam Store API. Use them to build game library browsers, playtime dashboards, achievement trackers, or friend activity panels. All hooks are read-only and built on the reactive `useAPI` base.

Auth: Get a Web API key at https://steamcommunity.com/dev/apikey. The key is sent as a query parameter (`key=...`). Note that some endpoints (friend list, recently played games) require the target profile to be set to public visibility.
sections.api:
--- useSteamUser ---

Fetch public profile information for a Steam user by their 64-bit SteamID.

  import { useSteamUser } from '@reactjit/apis/steam';

  const result: APIResult<{ response: { players: SteamPlayer[] } }> = useSteamUser(
    apiKey:  string | null,
    steamId: string | null,
  );

Parameters:
  apiKey   string | null   Steam Web API key. Pass null to disable.
  steamId  string | null   64-bit SteamID as a string. Pass null to disable.

Returns: APIResult<{ response: { players: SteamPlayer[] } }>
  data.response.players[0]  SteamPlayer   The requested player (array always has one entry)

SteamPlayer fields:
  steamid                  string
  personaname              string             Display name
  profileurl               string
  avatar                   string             32x32 avatar URL
  avatarmedium             string             64x64 avatar URL
  avatarfull               string             184x184 avatar URL
  personastate             number             Online state (0=offline, 1=online, 2=busy, 3=away, 4=snooze, 5=looking to trade, 6=looking to play)
  communityvisibilitystate number             1=private, 3=public
  lastlogoff               number | undefined Unix timestamp
  timecreated              number | undefined Account creation timestamp
  loccountrycode           string | undefined Two-letter country code
  locstatecode             string | undefined State/province code
  gameextrainfo            string | undefined Name of game currently being played
  gameid                   string | undefined App ID of game currently being played

--- useSteamOwnedGames ---

Fetch the full game library for a Steam user.

  import { useSteamOwnedGames } from '@reactjit/apis/steam';

  const result: APIResult<{ response: { game_count: number; games: SteamOwnedGame[] } }> = useSteamOwnedGames(
    apiKey:  string | null,
    steamId: string | null,
    opts?:   { includeAppInfo?: boolean },
  );

Parameters:
  apiKey                string | null   Steam API key. Pass null to disable.
  steamId               string | null   64-bit SteamID. Pass null to disable.
  opts.includeAppInfo   boolean         Include game name and icon hash in each entry. Defaults to true.

Returns: APIResult<{ response: { game_count: number; games: SteamOwnedGame[] } }>
  data.response.game_count  number
  data.response.games       SteamOwnedGame[]

SteamOwnedGame fields:
  appid                           number
  name                            string
  playtime_forever                number             Total playtime in minutes
  playtime_2weeks                 number | undefined Playtime in the last 2 weeks
  img_icon_url                    string             Icon hash — pass to steamGameIcon()
  has_community_visible_stats     boolean
  rtime_last_played               number             Unix timestamp of last session

--- useSteamRecentGames ---

Fetch the games a user has played in the last two weeks, sorted by recent playtime.

  import { useSteamRecentGames } from '@reactjit/apis/steam';

  const result: APIResult<{ response: { total_count: number; games: SteamRecentGame[] } }> = useSteamRecentGames(
    apiKey:  string | null,
    steamId: string | null,
    opts?:   { count?: number },
  );

Parameters:
  apiKey       string | null   Steam API key. Pass null to disable.
  steamId      string | null   64-bit SteamID. Pass null to disable.
  opts.count   number          Maximum number of games to return. Defaults to 10.

Returns: APIResult<{ response: { total_count: number; games: SteamRecentGame[] } }>
  data.response.total_count  number
  data.response.games        SteamRecentGame[]

SteamRecentGame fields:
  appid              number
  name               string
  playtime_2weeks    number   Minutes played in last 2 weeks
  playtime_forever   number   Total minutes played
  img_icon_url       string   Icon hash — pass to steamGameIcon()

--- useSteamFriends ---

Fetch the friend list for a Steam user. The profile must be public for this to succeed.

  import { useSteamFriends } from '@reactjit/apis/steam';

  const result: APIResult<{ friendslist: { friends: SteamFriend[] } }> = useSteamFriends(
    apiKey:  string | null,
    steamId: string | null,
  );

Parameters:
  apiKey   string | null
  steamId  string | null

Returns: APIResult<{ friendslist: { friends: SteamFriend[] } }>
  data.friendslist.friends  SteamFriend[]

SteamFriend fields:
  steamid       string
  relationship  string    Always 'friend' (only confirmed friends are returned)
  friend_since  number    Unix timestamp when the friendship was established

--- useSteamAchievements ---

Fetch the achievement status for a specific user and game combination.

  import { useSteamAchievements } from '@reactjit/apis/steam';

  const result: APIResult<{ playerstats: { achievements: SteamAchievement[] } }> = useSteamAchievements(
    apiKey:  string | null,
    steamId: string | null,
    appId:   number | null,
  );

Parameters:
  apiKey   string | null   Steam API key. Pass null to disable.
  steamId  string | null   64-bit SteamID. Pass null to disable.
  appId    number | null   Steam App ID (e.g. 570 for Dota 2). Pass null to disable.

Returns: APIResult<{ playerstats: { achievements: SteamAchievement[] } }>
  data.playerstats.achievements  SteamAchievement[]

SteamAchievement fields:
  apiname      string             Internal achievement identifier
  achieved     number             1 if unlocked, 0 if not
  unlocktime   number             Unix timestamp of unlock (0 if not achieved)
  name         string | undefined Human-readable name (included when the game schema is available)
  description  string | undefined Achievement description

--- useSteamAppDetails ---

Fetch store metadata for a game from the Steam Store API. No API key required.

  import { useSteamAppDetails } from '@reactjit/apis/steam';

  const result: APIResult<Record<string, { success: boolean; data: SteamAppDetails }>> = useSteamAppDetails(
    appId: number | null,
  );

Parameters:
  appId  number | null   Steam App ID. Pass null to disable.

Returns: APIResult<Record<string, { success: boolean; data: SteamAppDetails }>>
  data[appId].success   boolean
  data[appId].data      SteamAppDetails

SteamAppDetails fields:
  name                        string
  steam_appid                 number
  is_free                     boolean
  short_description           string
  header_image                string             Header image URL
  developers                  string[]
  publishers                  string[]
  price_overview              { final_formatted: string; discount_percent: number } | undefined
  metacritic                  { score: number; url: string } | undefined
  categories                  Array<{ description: string }>
  genres                      Array<{ description: string }>
  release_date                { coming_soon: boolean; date: string }

--- steamGameIcon (utility) ---

Construct a Steam CDN URL for a game's icon image.

  import { steamGameIcon } from '@reactjit/apis/steam';

  const url: string = steamGameIcon(appId: number, iconHash: string);

Parameters:
  appId      number   Steam App ID
  iconHash   string   The img_icon_url value from SteamOwnedGame or SteamRecentGame

Returns: string   Full JPEG URL

--- steamHeaderImage (utility) ---

Construct a Steam CDN URL for a game's 460x215 header image. No icon hash needed — only the App ID.

  import { steamHeaderImage } from '@reactjit/apis/steam';

  const url: string = steamHeaderImage(appId: number);

Parameters:
  appId  number   Steam App ID

Returns: string   Full JPEG URL for the store header image
seeAlso: hue — another local API with similar auth-in-URL-path pattern
useAPI — base hook used internally by all Steam hooks

metadata.title: Complete Style Property Reference
metadata.description: Every property in the Style interface with types, valid values, and descriptions

sections.overview:
The Style interface defines all visual and layout properties for ReactJIT nodes. It is passed via the `style` prop on Box, Text, Image, and other components. All properties are optional. Properties that are not set use their default values (typically 0, 'column', or undefined).

Colors can be CSS hex strings ("#ff0000", "#ff000080" with alpha), CSS function notation ("rgba(255, 0, 0, 0.5)"), or Love2D-style number arrays [r, g, b, a] where each value is 0-1.
sections.api:
--- SIZING ---

width?: number | string
  Width of the node. Number = pixels. String = percentage ("50%"), viewport ("10vw").
  Default: Inherits parent's width for non-text nodes. Text nodes auto-size.

height?: number | string
  Height of the node. Number = pixels. String = percentage, viewport ("5vh").
  Default: Auto-sizes to content.

minWidth?: number | string
  Minimum width constraint. Applied after flex distribution.
  Default: none

minHeight?: number | string
  Minimum height constraint. Applied after flex distribution.
  Default: none

maxWidth?: number | string
  Maximum width constraint. Applied after flex distribution.
  Default: none

maxHeight?: number | string
  Maximum height constraint. Applied after flex distribution.
  Default: none

aspectRatio?: number
  Width-to-height ratio. When one dimension is set, the other is computed.
  Example: aspectRatio: 16/9 means width = height * (16/9).
  Default: none

--- FLEXBOX ---

display?: 'flex' | 'none'
  'flex': Normal layout participation (default).
  'none': Node and all descendants skipped entirely from layout and painting.
  Default: 'flex'

flexDirection?: 'row' | 'column'
  Main axis direction. 'column' stacks children vertically. 'row' lays out horizontally.
  Default: 'column'

flexWrap?: 'nowrap' | 'wrap'
  'nowrap': All children on one line (default). 'wrap': Children wrap to next line.
  Default: 'nowrap'

justifyContent?: 'start' | 'center' | 'end' | 'space-between' | 'space-around' | 'space-evenly'
  Distribution of children along the main axis.
  Only effective when the container has a known main-axis size.
  Default: 'start'

alignItems?: 'start' | 'center' | 'end' | 'stretch'
  Alignment of children along the cross axis.
  'stretch': Children expand to fill the cross axis (unless they have explicit size).
  Default: 'stretch'

alignSelf?: 'auto' | 'start' | 'center' | 'end' | 'stretch'
  Per-child override of the parent's alignItems.
  'auto': Use parent's alignItems value.
  Default: 'auto'

flexGrow?: number
  Proportion of positive free space to absorb. Only works when the parent has
  definite main-axis size and there is leftover space after placing all children.
  Default: 0

flexShrink?: number
  Proportion of negative overflow to absorb. Activated when children overflow
  the parent's main axis. CSS default is 1 (items shrink proportionally).
  Default: 1

flexBasis?: number | string | 'auto'
  Initial main-axis size before flex distribution. Takes priority over width/height.
  'auto': Falls back to width (row) or height (column).
  Default: 'auto'

gap?: number | string
  Space between children on the main axis AND between lines when wrapping.
  Applied between items only (not before the first or after the last).
  Default: 0

--- SPACING ---

padding?: number | string
  Inner space on all four sides. Percentage resolves against the node's width.
  Default: 0

paddingLeft?: number | string
  Left inner space. Overrides padding for the left side.

paddingRight?: number | string
  Right inner space. Overrides padding for the right side.

paddingTop?: number | string
  Top inner space. Overrides padding for the top side.

paddingBottom?: number | string
  Bottom inner space. Overrides padding for the bottom side.

margin?: number | string
  Outer space on all four sides. Percentage resolves against the parent's width.
  Default: 0

marginLeft?: number | string
  Left outer space. Overrides margin for the left side.

marginRight?: number | string
  Right outer space. Overrides margin for the right side.

marginTop?: number | string
  Top outer space. Overrides margin for the top side.

marginBottom?: number | string
  Bottom outer space. Overrides margin for the bottom side.

--- VISUAL ---

backgroundColor?: Color
  Fill color for Box/View nodes. Hex string, rgba string, or [r,g,b,a] array.
  Default: transparent

borderRadius?: number
  Uniform corner radius in pixels. Applied to all four corners.
  Default: 0

borderTopLeftRadius?: number
  Per-corner override. Takes precedence over borderRadius.

borderTopRightRadius?: number
  Per-corner override. Takes precedence over borderRadius.

borderBottomLeftRadius?: number
  Per-corner override. Takes precedence over borderRadius.

borderBottomRightRadius?: number
  Per-corner override. Takes precedence over borderRadius.

borderWidth?: number
  Uniform border stroke width on all sides.
  Default: 0

borderTopWidth?: number
  Per-side border width. Overrides borderWidth for the top side.

borderRightWidth?: number
  Per-side border width. Overrides borderWidth for the right side.

borderBottomWidth?: number
  Per-side border width. Overrides borderWidth for the bottom side.

borderLeftWidth?: number
  Per-side border width. Overrides borderWidth for the left side.

borderColor?: Color
  Uniform border color on all sides.
  Default: gray (0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 1)

borderTopColor?: Color
  Per-side border color. Overrides borderColor for the top side.

borderRightColor?: Color
  Per-side border color. Overrides borderColor for the right side.

borderBottomColor?: Color
  Per-side border color. Overrides borderColor for the bottom side.

borderLeftColor?: Color
  Per-side border color. Overrides borderColor for the left side.

overflow?: 'visible' | 'hidden' | 'scroll'
  'visible': Content can extend beyond bounds (default).
  'hidden': Content clipped to bounds (stencil for rounded, scissor for rectangular).
  'scroll': Scrollable container with clipping and scroll indicators.
  Default: 'visible'

opacity?: number
  Node opacity (0-1). Multiplies with inherited opacity from parent chain.
  0 = fully transparent. 1 = fully opaque.
  Default: 1

zIndex?: number
  Paint order within the parent's children. Higher values paint on top.
  Does NOT affect layout, only paint order.
  Default: 0

scrollX?: number
  Horizontal scroll position for scroll containers (controlled mode).

scrollY?: number
  Vertical scroll position for scroll containers (controlled mode).

--- SHADOW ---

shadowColor?: Color
  Box shadow color. Shadow only renders when shadowBlur > 0.

shadowOffsetX?: number
  Horizontal shadow offset in pixels.
  Default: 0

shadowOffsetY?: number
  Vertical shadow offset in pixels.
  Default: 0

shadowBlur?: number
  Shadow blur radius. Simulated by rendering multiple fading rectangles.
  Capped at 10 blur steps for performance.
  Default: 0

--- GRADIENT ---

backgroundGradient?: { direction, colors }
  Linear gradient background. Takes precedence over backgroundColor.
  direction: 'horizontal' | 'vertical' | 'diagonal'
  colors: [Color, Color] -- start and end colors
  Rendered via Love2D mesh API on Love2D, CSS linear-gradient on web.

--- TRANSFORM ---

transform?: { translateX?, translateY?, rotate?, scaleX?, scaleY?, skewX?, skewY?, originX?, originY? }
  Visual transform. Does NOT affect layout or hit testing.
  translateX/Y: Pixel offset.
  rotate: Degrees (not radians).
  scaleX/Y: Scale factor (1 = no scale).
  skewX/Y: Skew angle in degrees.
  originX/Y: Transform origin as 0-1 fraction (0.5 = center, default).

--- TEXT ---

color?: Color
  Text color. Applied to Text and __TEXT__ nodes.
  Inherited by __TEXT__ children from parent Text nodes.
  Default: white (1, 1, 1, 1)

fontSize?: number
  Font size in pixels. REQUIRED on all Text nodes (enforced by linter).
  Inherited by __TEXT__ children from parent Text nodes.
  Default: 14 (but the linter requires explicit values)

fontFamily?: string
  Path to a .ttf or .otf font file. Relative to the Love2D filesystem root.
  Inherited by __TEXT__ children from parent Text nodes.
  Default: Love2D default font

fontWeight?: 'normal' | 'bold' | number
  Font weight. 'bold' triggers faux-bold rendering (double draw with offset).
  Default: 'normal'

textAlign?: 'left' | 'center' | 'right'
  Horizontal text alignment within the node's computed width.
  Default: 'left'

textOverflow?: 'clip' | 'ellipsis'
  How to handle text that overflows the node's width.
  'ellipsis': Truncate with "..." at the end.
  Default: 'clip'

textDecorationLine?: 'none' | 'underline' | 'line-through'
  Text decoration line style.
  Default: 'none'

lineHeight?: number
  Line height in pixels. Overrides the font's natural line height.
  When set, text is rendered line-by-line with this spacing.
  Default: Font's natural line height

letterSpacing?: number
  Extra space between characters in pixels.
  WARNING: Activates character-by-character rendering. Expensive on large text.
  Default: 0

--- TEXT SHADOW ---

textShadowColor?: Color
  Text shadow color. Shadow only renders when this is set.

textShadowOffsetX?: number
  Horizontal text shadow offset.
  Default: 0

textShadowOffsetY?: number
  Vertical text shadow offset.
  Default: 0

--- IMAGE ---

objectFit?: 'fill' | 'contain' | 'cover' | 'none'
  How the image scales within its bounds.
  'fill': Stretch to fill (default). 'contain': Fit inside, letterbox.
  'cover': Fill, crop overflow. 'none': No scaling, center.
  Default: 'fill'

--- POSITIONING ---

position?: 'relative' | 'absolute'
  'relative': Normal flow (default).
  'absolute': Positioned relative to the nearest positioned ancestor.
  Default: 'relative'

top?: number | string
  Offset from the top edge (absolute positioning).

bottom?: number | string
  Offset from the bottom edge (absolute positioning).

left?: number | string
  Offset from the left edge (absolute positioning).

right?: number | string
  Offset from the right edge (absolute positioning).

--- VISIBILITY ---

visibility?: 'visible' | 'hidden'
  'visible': Normal rendering (default).
  'hidden': Node is not painted, but still occupies layout space.
  Unlike display:'none', children of a hidden node still paint.
  Default: 'visible'

--- OUTLINE ---

outlineColor?: Color
  Outline color. Drawn outside the border box.
  Default: white (1, 1, 1, 1) or currentColor on web

outlineWidth?: number
  Outline stroke width.
  Default: 0

outlineOffset?: number
  Gap between the border box and the outline.
  Default: 0

--- TRANSITION ---

transition?: { [property: string]: { duration?, easing?, delay? } }
  CSS-style transitions. Lua-side: JS declares transition targets, Lua interpolates.
  Web-side: Mapped directly to CSS transition property.

  Per-property: transition: { backgroundColor: { duration: 300, easing: 'easeInOut' } }
  All properties: transition: { all: { duration: 300 } }

  duration: number in milliseconds (default: 300)
  easing: 'linear' | 'easeIn' | 'easeOut' | 'easeInOut' | 'bounce' | 'elastic' (default: 'easeInOut')
  delay: number in milliseconds (default: 0)

--- ANIMATION ---

animation?: { keyframes, duration?, easing?, iterations?, direction?, fillMode?, delay?, playState? }
  CSS-style keyframe animations. Runs on the Lua side.

  keyframes: { [percentage: number]: Partial<Style> }
    Percentage keys from 0 to 100. Each value is a partial Style object.
    Example: { 0: { opacity: 0 }, 100: { opacity: 1 } }

  duration: number in milliseconds (default: 300)
  easing: string easing function name (default: 'linear')
  iterations: number, -1 = infinite (default: 1)
  direction: 'normal' | 'reverse' | 'alternate' | 'alternate-reverse' (default: 'normal')
  fillMode: 'none' | 'forwards' | 'backwards' | 'both' (default: 'none')
  delay: number in milliseconds (default: 0)
  playState: 'running' | 'paused' (default: 'running')

metadata.title: Telegram Bot API
metadata.description: Hooks for fetching bot identity, polling updates, and sending messages or photos through a Telegram bot.

sections.overview:
The Telegram hooks wrap the official Telegram Bot API. They cover three concerns: identifying the bot (`useTelegramBot`), polling for incoming messages and callback queries (`useTelegramUpdates`), and sending messages or photos imperatively (`useTelegramSend`).

Auth: Create a bot via @BotFather in Telegram. BotFather gives you a token string of the form `1234567890:ABCDEFGHIJabcdefghij`. Pass it as `botToken` to every hook. The token is embedded directly in the API URL path — treat it as a secret.
sections.api:
--- useTelegramBot ---

Fetch the identity of the bot associated with the given token. Use this to confirm the token is valid and to get the bot's username and capability flags.

  import { useTelegramBot } from '@reactjit/apis/telegram';

  const result: APIResult<{ ok: boolean; result: TelegramBotInfo }> = useTelegramBot(
    botToken: string | null,
  );

Parameters:
  botToken  string | null   Telegram bot token from BotFather. Pass null to disable.

Returns: APIResult<{ ok: boolean; result: TelegramBotInfo }>
  data.ok      boolean
  data.result  TelegramBotInfo

TelegramBotInfo fields:
  id                            number
  is_bot                        boolean    Always true for bots
  first_name                    string     Bot display name
  username                      string     Bot username (without @)
  can_join_groups               boolean
  can_read_all_group_messages   boolean
  supports_inline_queries       boolean

--- useTelegramUpdates ---

Poll for incoming updates (messages, edited messages, callback queries) using Telegram's long-polling `getUpdates` method. Refetches on a configurable interval.

  import { useTelegramUpdates } from '@reactjit/apis/telegram';

  const result: APIResult<{ ok: boolean; result: TelegramUpdate[] }> = useTelegramUpdates(
    botToken: string | null,
    opts?:    { offset?: number; limit?: number; interval?: number },
  );

Parameters:
  botToken       string | null   Telegram bot token. Pass null to disable.
  opts.offset    number          Return only updates with update_id >= offset. Pass the last update_id + 1 to acknowledge processed updates.
  opts.limit     number          Maximum number of updates to return per poll. Defaults to 20. Max 100.
  opts.interval  number          Polling interval in milliseconds. Defaults to 5000 (5 seconds).

Returns: APIResult<{ ok: boolean; result: TelegramUpdate[] }>
  data.ok      boolean
  data.result  TelegramUpdate[]

TelegramUpdate fields:
  update_id        number                      Globally unique update ID. Use for offset tracking.
  message          TelegramMessage | undefined  A new incoming message
  edited_message   TelegramMessage | undefined  An edited message
  callback_query   object | undefined           A callback from an inline keyboard button

TelegramMessage fields:
  message_id           number
  from                 TelegramUser | undefined
  chat                 TelegramChat
  date                 number                   Unix timestamp
  text                 string | undefined
  photo                Array<{ file_id, width, height }> | undefined
  document             { file_id, file_name?, mime_type? } | undefined
  reply_to_message     TelegramMessage | undefined

TelegramChat fields:
  id          number
  type        'private' | 'group' | 'supergroup' | 'channel'
  title       string | undefined
  username    string | undefined
  first_name  string | undefined
  last_name   string | undefined

TelegramUser fields:
  id              number
  is_bot          boolean
  first_name      string
  last_name       string | undefined
  username        string | undefined
  language_code   string | undefined

--- useTelegramSend ---

Imperative mutation hook for sending messages and photos to any chat your bot has access to.

  import { useTelegramSend } from '@reactjit/apis/telegram';

  const sender = useTelegramSend(botToken: string | null);

Parameters:
  botToken  string | null   Telegram bot token. Pass null to disable (all methods reject with an error).

Returns: object
  sendMessage   (chatId, text, opts?) => Promise<any>
  sendPhoto     (chatId, photoUrl, opts?) => Promise<any>
  loading       boolean   True while any mutation request is in flight
  error         Error | null

sendMessage signature:
  sendMessage(
    chatId:            number | string,
    text:              string,
    opts?: {
      parseMode?:         'HTML' | 'Markdown' | 'MarkdownV2';
      replyToMessageId?:  number;
    },
  ): Promise<any>

  chatId              number | string   Telegram chat ID or @username
  text                string            Message text
  opts.parseMode      string            Enable HTML or Markdown formatting in text
  opts.replyToMessageId  number         Reply to a specific message by ID

sendPhoto signature:
  sendPhoto(
    chatId:   number | string,
    photoUrl: string,
    opts?: {
      caption?: string;
    },
  ): Promise<any>

  chatId      number | string   Target chat ID
  photoUrl    string            Public URL of the image to send
  opts.caption  string          Optional caption text shown below the photo
seeAlso: hue — another token-in-URL-path API with similar auth pattern
useAPIMutation — low-level imperative mutation hook used internally by useTelegramSend

metadata.title: TMDB API Hooks
metadata.description: Hooks for The Movie Database API — trending, search, movie details, series details, and popular titles.

sections.overview:
The TMDB hooks wrap The Movie Database (TMDB) REST API v3. Authentication uses an API key passed as a query parameter — get one at https://www.themoviedb.org/settings/api. Pass the key as the first argument to every hook. A null key skips the request.

All hooks return APIResult<T> with data, loading, error, and refetch. Paginated endpoints return TMDBPaginated<T> which includes the current page, total pages, and total results.

The package also exports a tmdbImage() utility for constructing full image URLs from the partial paths the API returns.
sections.api:
--- useTMDBTrending ---

Fetch trending movies or TV shows for today or this week.

  import { useTMDBTrending } from '@reactjit/apis/tmdb';

  const result: APIResult<TMDBPaginated<TMDBMovie>> = useTMDBTrending(apiKey, opts?);

Parameters:
  apiKey   string | null
  opts     object (optional)
    mediaType   'movie' | 'tv' | 'all'    Default: 'movie'
    timeWindow  'day' | 'week'            Default: 'week'

Returns: APIResult<TMDBPaginated<TMDBMovie>>
  data.page           number
  data.results        TMDBMovie[]
  data.total_pages    number
  data.total_results  number

--- useTMDBSearch ---

Search the TMDB catalog by text query. Supports movies, TV shows, or a combined multi-search.

  import { useTMDBSearch } from '@reactjit/apis/tmdb';

  const result: APIResult<TMDBPaginated<TMDBMovie & TMDBSeries>> = useTMDBSearch(apiKey, query, opts?);

Parameters:
  apiKey   string | null
  query    string | null   Pass null to skip (e.g. while the user is typing).
  opts     object (optional)
    type   'movie' | 'tv' | 'multi'   Default: 'multi'
    page   number                      Default: none (API default: 1)

Returns: APIResult<TMDBPaginated<TMDBMovie & TMDBSeries>>

--- useTMDBMovie ---

Fetch full details for a single movie, including runtime, budget, genres, and production companies.

  import { useTMDBMovie } from '@reactjit/apis/tmdb';

  const result: APIResult<TMDBMovieDetails> = useTMDBMovie(apiKey, movieId);

Parameters:
  apiKey    string | null
  movieId   number | null   TMDB movie ID. Pass null to skip.

Returns: APIResult<TMDBMovieDetails>

TMDBMovieDetails extends TMDBMovie and adds:
  runtime               number
  budget                number
  revenue               number
  tagline               string
  genres                Array<{ id: number; name: string }>
  production_companies  Array<{ id: number; name: string; logo_path: string | null }>
  imdb_id               string | null

--- useTMDBSeries ---

Fetch full details for a single TV series, including season count, episode count, and networks.

  import { useTMDBSeries } from '@reactjit/apis/tmdb';

  const result: APIResult<TMDBSeriesDetails> = useTMDBSeries(apiKey, seriesId);

Parameters:
  apiKey     string | null
  seriesId   number | null   TMDB series ID. Pass null to skip.

Returns: APIResult<TMDBSeriesDetails>

TMDBSeriesDetails extends TMDBSeries and adds:
  number_of_seasons    number
  number_of_episodes   number
  episode_run_time     number[]
  genres               Array<{ id: number; name: string }>
  status               string
  networks             Array<{ id: number; name: string; logo_path: string | null }>

--- useTMDBPopular ---

Fetch the current popular movies or TV shows.

  import { useTMDBPopular } from '@reactjit/apis/tmdb';

  const result: APIResult<TMDBPaginated<TMDBMovie>> = useTMDBPopular(apiKey, opts?);

Parameters:
  apiKey   string | null
  opts     object (optional)
    type   'movie' | 'tv'   Default: 'movie'
    page   number           Default: none (API default: 1)

Returns: APIResult<TMDBPaginated<TMDBMovie>>

--- tmdbImage ---

Construct a full image URL from the partial path returned by TMDB.

  import { tmdbImage } from '@reactjit/apis/tmdb';

  const url: string | null = tmdbImage(path, size?);

Parameters:
  path   string | null   The poster_path or backdrop_path value from any TMDB type.
  size   string          TMDB image size code. Default: 'w500'. Common: 'w92', 'w185', 'w342', 'w500', 'w780', 'original'.

Returns: string | null — null if path is null or empty.
seeAlso: trakt — alternative watch history and media tracking (see packages/apis/src/trakt.ts)

metadata.title: Todoist Hooks
metadata.description: Hooks for reading tasks, projects, sections, and labels from Todoist, plus imperative mutations for creating, completing, and deleting tasks.

sections.overview:
The Todoist hooks in @reactjit/apis use the Todoist REST API v2. Authentication is a single Bearer token found under Settings > Integrations > Developer in the Todoist web app.

Read hooks (useTodoistTasks, useTodoistProjects, etc.) are reactive — they fetch on mount and expose a refetch() method. The useTodoistMutation hook returns imperative functions for write operations: creating tasks, marking them complete, reopening, updating, and deleting.

All read hooks accept null as the token argument to suppress fetching, which is the standard pattern for conditional data loading in ReactJIT.
sections.api:
--- useTodoistTasks ---

Fetch tasks with optional filtering by project, label, or Todoist filter expression.

  import { useTodoistTasks } from '@reactjit/apis';

  const result: APIResult<TodoistTask[]> = useTodoistTasks(
    token: string | null,
    opts?: { projectId?: string; label?: string; filter?: string },
  );

Parameters:
  token  string | null   Todoist API token
  opts   object | undefined
    projectId  string    Filter to a specific project ID
    label      string    Filter to a specific label name
    filter     string    Todoist filter query string (e.g. 'today | overdue')

Returns: APIResult<TodoistTask[]>

TodoistTask fields:
  id             string
  content        string              Task title
  description    string              Additional notes
  is_completed   boolean
  priority       1 | 2 | 3 | 4      1 = normal, 4 = urgent
  due            object | null
    date         string              Due date in YYYY-MM-DD format
    string       string              Human-readable due string
