Text is the primitive for rendering text content. It is the only element that carries intrinsic width -- the layout engine measures the text string against the specified font and fontSize to determine how much horizontal space it occupies. This makes Text unique among primitives: it is the only element that can size itself without an explicit width prop.

Text supports font styling (fontSize, fontFamily, fontWeight, color, textAlign, letterSpacing, lineHeight), text truncation via numberOfLines, and keyboard event handlers. In web mode it renders as a `<span>`; in native mode it outputs a `'Text'` host element for the Lua painter.
sections.api:
```tsx
import { Text } from '@reactjit/core';
```

```tsx
<Text style={{ fontSize: 16, color: '#cdd6f4' }}>
  Hello, world
</Text>
```

Props:
| Prop          | Type                         | Default   | Description                              |
|---------------|------------------------------|-----------|------------------------------------------|
| style         | Style                        | undefined | Text styling (fontSize, color, etc.)     |
| numberOfLines | number                       | undefined | Truncate text to N lines with ellipsis   |
| onKeyDown     | (event: LoveEvent) => void   | undefined | Key pressed while focused                |
| onKeyUp       | (event: LoveEvent) => void   | undefined | Key released while focused               |
| onTextInput   | (event: LoveEvent) => void   | undefined | Text input while focused                 |
| children      | ReactNode                    | undefined | Text content (strings, numbers, etc.)    |

Relevant Style properties for Text:
| Property           | Type                              | Description                              |
|--------------------|-----------------------------------|------------------------------------------|
| fontSize           | number                            | Font size in pixels (REQUIRED)           |
| color              | Color                             | Text color                               |
| fontFamily         | string                            | Font family name                         |
| fontWeight         | 'normal' | 'bold' | number        | Font weight                              |
| textAlign          | 'left' | 'center' | 'right'       | Horizontal text alignment                |
| textOverflow       | 'clip' | 'ellipsis'               | How overflowing text is handled          |
| textDecorationLine | 'none' | 'underline' | 'line-through' | Text decoration                    |
| lineHeight         | number                            | Line height in pixels                    |
| letterSpacing      | number                            | Extra space between characters            |
| textShadowColor    | Color                             | Text shadow color                        |
| textShadowOffsetX  | number                            | Text shadow horizontal offset            |
| textShadowOffsetY  | number                            | Text shadow vertical offset              |

examples.title: Basic text
examples.code:
```tsx
<Text style={{ fontSize: 16, color: '#cdd6f4' }}>
  Hello, world
</Text>
```

examples.title: Styled heading
examples.code:
```tsx
<Text style={{
  fontSize: 32,
  fontWeight: 'bold',
  color: '#89b4fa',
  textAlign: 'center',
  letterSpacing: 1.2,
}}>
  Dashboard
</Text>
```

examples.title: Truncated text with numberOfLines
examples.code:
```tsx
<Box style={{ width: 200 }}>
  <Text style={{ fontSize: 14, color: '#a6adc8' }} numberOfLines={2}>
    This is a long paragraph of text that will be truncated to two lines
    with an ellipsis at the end if it overflows the available space.
  </Text>
</Box>
```

examples.title: Dynamic content with template literal
Text with shadow
examples.code:
```tsx
<Text style={{
  fontSize: 24,
  color: '#cdd6f4',
  textShadowColor: [0, 0, 0, 0.5],
  textShadowOffsetX: 2,
  textShadowOffsetY: 2,
}}>
  Shadow Text
</Text>
```

platformNotes.Love2D: Renders as a 'Text' host element. Font measurement happens in Lua via love.graphics.getFont():getWidth(). The painter draws text with love.graphics.print(). Custom fonts are loaded by fontFamily name from the project's font directory. Text wrapping is computed by the layout engine when the parent constrains width.
platformNotes.Web: Renders as a <span> with display: inline. The styleToCSS() converter maps fontSize, color, fontFamily, and other text properties to their CSS equivalents. numberOfLines uses -webkit-line-clamp for multi-line truncation with ellipsis.
platformNotes.Terminal: Grid targets use <text> lowercase intrinsic. Character-grid rendering means fontSize has no effect; text occupies cells based on character count. Color maps to ANSI truecolor or palette indices depending on the target.

criticalRules: EVERY Text element MUST have an explicit fontSize in its style prop. The static linter (`reactjit lint`) enforces this as a build-blocking error. Text without fontSize will produce unpredictable rendering because the layout engine cannot measure the text.
Do not mix bare text and JSX expressions: `"count: {value}"` renders literally as that string. Use template literals instead: `{`count: ${value}`}`.
Text is the only primitive with intrinsic width. Box and Image have zero width unless you set one explicitly. This means Text can size itself and its parent, but Box cannot.
Do not nest Box inside Text. Text children should be strings, numbers, or other Text elements only.

Do not use the block character (U+2588) inside Text. It renders as a font glyph, not a filled pixel. Use colored Box elements with backgroundColor instead. The linter enforces this via `no-block-char-in-text`.

metadata.title: TextEditor
metadata.description: Multi-line document editor with Lua-owned interaction for native mode and textarea for web.

sections.overview:
TextEditor is a multi-line editing surface designed for document-style text editing. Unlike TextInput, which handles per-keystroke editing in JavaScript, TextEditor's native mode delegates ALL editing interaction (cursor movement, selection, keystrokes, scrolling) to Lua. The JavaScript side only receives boundary events: focus, blur, and submit.

This "Lua-owned interaction" design eliminates bridge traffic for every keystroke, making TextEditor suitable for editing large text content in the Love2D target. In web mode, TextEditor renders a standard HTML `<textarea>` with monospace styling.

TextEditor can be integrated with ReactJIT's spell checking system for inline spell checking with dictionary support. Spell checking is implemented at the application level using the bridge RPC API (`spell:check`, `spell:checkText`, `spell:suggest`).
sections.api:
Import: import { TextEditor } from '@reactjit/core';

Usage:
```tsx
<TextEditor
  initialValue="Hello, world!"
  onBlur={(text) => saveDocument(text)}
  style={{ width: 400, height: 300 }}
/>
```

Props (from TextEditorProps in types.ts):

| Prop          | Type                       | Default | Description                                         |
|---------------|----------------------------|---------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| initialValue  | string                     | -       | Initial text content used on first render            |
| value         | string                     | -       | Controlled value -- updates editor text when changed |
| onChangeText  | (text: string) => void     | -       | Called on blur/submit with the final text value      |
| onSubmit      | (text: string) => void     | -       | Called on Ctrl+Enter with the current text value     |
| onFocus       | () => void                 | -       | Called when the editor gains focus                   |
| onBlur        | (text: string) => void     | -       | Called when the editor loses focus, with final text  |
| placeholder   | string                     | -       | Placeholder text shown when empty and unfocused      |
| readOnly      | boolean                    | false   | Whether the editor is read-only                      |
| lineNumbers   | boolean                    | true    | Whether to show line numbers in the gutter           |
| style         | Style                      | -       | Container style (sizing, layout)                     |
| textStyle     | Style                      | -       | Text style (fontSize, color, fontFamily)             |

examples.title: Basic editor with blur handler
examples.code:
```tsx
<TextEditor
  initialValue="function hello()\n  print('world')\nend"
  onBlur={(text) => console.log('Final text:', text)}
  style={{ width: '100%', height: 200 }}
/>
```

examples.title: Controlled editor
Read-only code viewer
Editor with spell checking integration

criticalRules: TextEditor needs explicit `width` and `height` in its style. Without sizing, the editor container will collapse.
In native mode, `onChangeText` is NOT called on every keystroke. It fires only on blur and submit boundary events. This is by design to avoid bridge traffic.
The `onBlur` callback signature is `(text: string) => void`, unlike TextInput's `() => void`. TextEditor passes the final text value because Lua owns the editing state.
`initialValue` is only used on the first render. To update the text programmatically after mount, use the controlled `value` prop.
Submit is triggered by Ctrl+Enter, not plain Enter (which inserts a newline).

Spell checking integration requires the bridge API and dictionary setup. It is not automatic -- you must explicitly call `bridge.rpc('spell:checkText', ...)` or `bridge.rpc('spell:check', ...)` to check spelling. Spell check functionality is only available in native (Love2D) mode.

metadata.title: TextInput
metadata.description: Editable single-line or multiline text field with cursor, selection, clipboard, and secure entry support.

sections.overview:
TextInput is an editable text field that handles keyboard input, cursor positioning, selection, and text manipulation. It supports single-line and multiline modes, placeholder text, secure entry (password masking), clipboard operations, and both controlled and uncontrolled value management.

In web mode, TextInput renders a native HTML `<input>` or `<textarea>` with standard browser behavior. In native mode (Love2D), it renders a Lua-owned custom implementation where ALL editing state (text, cursor position, selection, clipboard) lives entirely in Lua. Only boundary events (focus, blur, submit, changeText) cross the JS bridge — there is zero bridge traffic per keystroke.
sections.api:
Import: import { TextInput } from '@reactjit/core';

Usage:
```tsx
<TextInput
  value={text}
  onChangeText={setText}
  placeholder="Type here..."
  style={{ width: 200 }}
/>
```

Props (from TextInputProps in types.ts):

| Prop             | Type                       | Default     | Description                                        |
|------------------|----------------------------|-------------|----------------------------------------------------|
| value            | string                     | -           | Controlled text value                              |
| defaultValue     | string                     | -           | Initial value in uncontrolled mode                 |
| onChangeText     | (text: string) => void     | -           | Called when text changes (blur/submit in native)   |
| onSubmit         | (text: string) => void     | -           | Called on Enter (single-line) or submit action     |
| onFocus          | () => void                 | -           | Called when the input gains focus                  |
| onBlur           | () => void                 | -           | Called when the input loses focus                  |
| placeholder      | string                     | -           | Placeholder text shown when empty                  |
| placeholderColor | Color                      | -           | Color of placeholder text (native: '#888888')      |
| maxLength        | number                     | -           | Maximum character count                            |
| multiline        | boolean                    | false       | Enables multiline mode (textarea)                  |
| editable         | boolean                    | true        | Whether text can be edited                         |
| secureTextEntry  | boolean                    | false       | Masks text with bullet characters                  |
| keyboardType     | string                     | 'default'   | Hint for on-screen keyboard (numeric, email, etc.) |
| style            | Style                      | -           | Container style overrides                          |
| textStyle        | Style                      | -           | Text content style (fontSize, color, etc.)         |
| autoFocus        | boolean                    | false       | Automatically focus on mount                       |
| cursorColor      | Color                      | -           | Color of the blinking cursor and focus border      |

examples.title: Basic controlled input
Password field
Multiline with max length

criticalRules: TextInput needs explicit `width` in its style. Without it, the container will collapse.
In native mode, cursor position and selection are maintained entirely in Lua. External `value` changes will clamp the cursor to the new text length and clear any active selection.
The `onSubmit` callback fires on Enter in single-line mode. In multiline mode, Enter inserts a newline instead.
In native mode, `onChangeText` is invoked only on blur or submit (boundary events), not per keystroke. For real-time text updates, consider using TextEditor or implement a custom bridge event.
When `editable` is false, the text is displayed but keystroke events are ignored.
`secureTextEntry` replaces each character with a bullet character (U+2022) in the display. The actual text is preserved in the internal state.
Native mode requires explicit `fontSize` in `textStyle` or `style` for proper font rendering and cursor positioning. The linter enforces this for Text components; apply the same rule for TextInput.
Selection highlighting in native mode uses a semi-transparent blue overlay (`rgba(74, 144, 217, 0.3)`). The selection range is tracked as byte offsets in the UTF-8 encoded text.

metadata.title: Toolbar
metadata.description: Horizontal toolbar with action items and dividers for grouping commands.

sections.overview:
Toolbar renders a horizontal row of action items with optional dividers for visual grouping. Each item is a labeled pressable button. Dividers are thin vertical lines that separate logical groups.

Items are provided as an array of ToolbarEntry objects — either { type: 'item' } with an id, label, and optional disabled flag, or { type: 'divider' } for visual separation.

examples.title: Basic toolbar
examples.code:
<Toolbar
  items={[
    { type: 'item', id: 'save', label: 'Save' },
    { type: 'item', id: 'undo', label: 'Undo' },
    { type: 'divider' },
    { type: 'item', id: 'settings', label: 'Settings' },
  ]}
  onSelect={(id) => console.log('Selected:', id)}
/>

examples.title: Toolbar with disabled items
examples.code:
<Toolbar
  items={[
    { type: 'item', id: 'cut', label: 'Cut', disabled: true },
    { type: 'item', id: 'copy', label: 'Copy' },
    { type: 'item', id: 'paste', label: 'Paste' },
  ]}
  onSelect={handleAction}
/>

examples.title: Toolbar with custom styling
examples.code:
<Toolbar
  items={[
    { type: 'item', id: 'play', label: 'Play' },
    { type: 'divider' },
    { type: 'item', id: 'stop', label: 'Stop' },
  ]}
  onSelect={handleTransport}
  style={{ backgroundColor: '#1a1a2e', borderRadius: 8, padding: 6 }}
/>

platformNotes.Love2D:
Items render as Pressable elements with hover/press state styling.
Dividers render as thin Box elements with backgroundColor.
platformNotes.Web: Same rendering as Love2D — Pressable maps to interactive div elements.

criticalRules: Every item entry must have a unique `id` — this is the value passed to `onSelect`.
Disabled items render with reduced opacity and do not trigger `onSelect`.
Dividers are purely visual — they have no id and produce no events.
All Text inside Toolbar items must have explicit `fontSize` (the component handles this internally).
Toolbar auto-sizes to fit its items. To constrain width, wrap in a Box or set `style.width`.
seeAlso: Tabs -- Tab navigation for switching views
NavPanel -- Sidebar navigation with grouped sections

metadata.title: Video
metadata.description: Plays video files with hardware acceleration via libmpv

sections.overview:
Video plays video files in any format that libmpv supports (MP4, MKV, WebM, AVI, MOV, OGV, etc.) with hardware-accelerated decoding. Unlike the old FFmpeg transcoding pipeline, videos play instantly without any pre-processing step.

In Love2D native mode, Video renders via libmpv's OpenGL render API, which creates video frames directly into a Love2D Canvas using an isolated framebuffer pipeline. This means videos render at their native resolution and scale to fit the container via the objectFit property — the same as Image.

In web mode, Video renders as an `<video>` element with standard HTML5 controls and streaming support.

The Video component supports play/pause control, volume, mute, seeking, looping, and playback events (onPlay, onPause, onTimeUpdate, onEnded).

Additionally, any Box component can display background or hover videos via the backgroundVideo and hoverVideo props, enabling effects like video backgrounds and interactive hover previews.

Requires: libmpv-dev (on Love2D). If libmpv is not installed, videos show an error placeholder.
sections.api:
```tsx
import { Video } from '@reactjit/core';
```

```tsx
<Video
  src="assets/demo.mp4"
  paused={false}
  volume={0.8}
  muted={false}
  loop={false}
  style={{ width: 640, height: 360, objectFit: 'contain' }}
  onPlay={() => console.log('playing')}
  onPause={() => console.log('paused')}
  onTimeUpdate={(e) => console.log(`Time: ${e.currentTime}s`)}
  onEnded={() => console.log('finished')}
/>
```

Props:
| Prop         | Type                                    | Default   | Description                              |
|--------------|-----------------------------------------|-----------|------------------------------------------|
| src          | string                                  | REQUIRED  | Video file path or URL (HTTP/HTTPS OK)   |
| paused       | boolean                                 | false     | Control playback pause state             |
| volume       | number (0-1)                            | 1         | Audio volume multiplier                  |
| muted        | boolean                                 | false     | Mute audio                               |
| loop         | boolean                                 | false     | Loop playback at end                     |
| w            | number \| string                        | undefined | Shorthand for width                      |
| h            | number \| string                        | undefined | Shorthand for height                     |
| radius       | number                                  | undefined | Shorthand for borderRadius               |
| style        | Style                                   | undefined | Sizing and visual styling                |
| onPlay       | () => void                              | undefined | Fires when video starts playing          |
| onPause      | () => void                              | undefined | Fires when video is paused               |
| onTimeUpdate | (e: {currentTime, duration}) => void    | undefined | Fires ~4x per second during playback     |
| onEnded      | () => void                              | undefined | Fires when video reaches the end         |
| onReady      | () => void                              | undefined | Fires when video is loaded and ready     |
| onError      | (e: {message: string}) => void          | undefined | Fires on playback error                  |
| onClick      | (e: LoveEvent) => void                  | undefined | Fires on click/tap                       |

objectFit values (set via style.objectFit):
| Value   | Behavior                                                        |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------------------|
| fill    | Stretch video to fill the element (may distort aspect ratio)    |
| contain | Scale video to fit entirely within the element (letterboxed)    |
| cover   | Scale video to cover the element entirely (may crop)            |
| none    | Display at original size, no scaling                            |

Box Video Props (for background and hover videos):
| Prop                | Type                           | Default   | Description                                      |
|---------------------|--------------------------------|-----------|--------------------------------------------------|
| backgroundVideo     | string                         | undefined | Video src to render as background layer          |
| backgroundVideoFit  | 'fill' \| 'contain' \| 'cover' | 'cover'   | How background video scales to Box dimensions    |
| hoverVideo          | string                         | undefined | Video src to play when Box is hovered            |
| hoverVideoFit       | 'fill' \| 'contain' \| 'cover' | 'cover'   | How hover video scales to Box dimensions         |

examples.title: Basic video with fixed size
examples.code:
```tsx
<Video
  src="assets/intro.mp4"
  style={{ width: 640, height: 360 }}
/>
```

examples.title: Video with playback controls
Video in a responsive card with border radius
examples.code:
```tsx
<Box
  style={{
    width: 400,
    borderRadius: 12,
    overflow: 'hidden',
    backgroundColor: '#1a1a1a',
  }}
>
  <Video
    src="assets/clip.mp4"
    loop={true}
    style={{ width: '100%', height: 225, objectFit: 'cover' }}
  />
  <Box style={{ padding: 16 }}>
    <Text style={{ fontSize: 18, fontWeight: 'bold' }}>Demo Video</Text>
  </Box>
</Box>
```

examples.title: Looping background video with time display
Box with background video (fullscreen)
examples.code:
```tsx
<Box
  fill
  backgroundVideo="assets/menu-background.mp4"
  backgroundVideoFit="cover"
>
  {/* Gradient overlay for readability */}
  <Box fill style={{ backgroundColor: '#00000066' }}>
    <Box fill style={{ justifyContent: 'center', alignItems: 'center' }}>
      <Text style={{ fontSize: 48, color: '#fff', fontWeight: 'bold' }}>
        WELCOME
      </Text>
    </Box>
  </Box>
</Box>
```

examples.title: Box with hover video (interactive preview card)

platformNotes.Love2D:
Renders via libmpv's OpenGL render API with hardware-accelerated video decoding (hwdec=auto-safe). Videos load instantly into a private OpenGL framebuffer and are blitted to a Love2D Canvas each frame via glBlitFramebuffer. No transcoding step. The src path is relative to the project root. Supported formats: any format libmpv supports (MP4, MKV, WebM, AVI, MOV, OGV, H.264, VP8, VP9, AV1, etc.).
Requires libmpv-dev to be installed on the system. If libmpv is not available, the Video component will display an error placeholder. Install with: apt install libmpv-dev
Audio plays via libmpv's native audio output (PulseAudio, ALSA, or null if muted). Volume and mute control the mpv audio properties directly.
HTTP/HTTPS URLs are supported directly — mpv will stream from the URL without pre-downloading. M3U8/HLS streams are supported (VOD mode only).
GL state isolation: mpv renders to its own private FBO, and all OpenGL state (17 variables including bound FBO, viewport, scissor, program, etc.) is saved and restored around mpv render calls to prevent GL corruption in the Love2D rendering pipeline. The library is loaded with RTLD_DEEPBIND to isolate mpv's embedded Lua 5.2 symbols from Love2D's LuaJIT.
backgroundVideo and hoverVideo props on Box components are implemented using the same mpv pipeline. Background videos loop automatically and play muted. Hover videos play only while hovered and automatically pause when the cursor leaves.
platformNotes.Web:
Renders as an `<video>` element with standard HTML5 video controls. The browser's native decoder is used. src can be a relative path, absolute URL, or blob URL.
backgroundVideo and hoverVideo on Box components are rendered as absolutely positioned `<video>` elements with pointer-events: none, layered behind the Box content.
Terminal / ComputerCraft / Neovim / AwesomeWM:
Video is not supported on character-grid targets. These platforms cannot display raster video. Use a placeholder Box or progress indicator instead.

criticalRules: Video has no intrinsic size. You must provide width and height via the style prop (or w/h shorthand), or constrain the video via a parent Box with known dimensions. A Video without sizing will collapse to zero.
The src prop is required. Omitting it will produce an error.
Video paths in Love2D are relative to the project root directory (where main.lua lives), or can be absolute paths or HTTP/HTTPS URLs.
Video accepts no children. It is a leaf element.
On Love2D, changing the src prop while a video is playing will stop playback and load the new video.
The paused, volume, muted, and loop props are reactive. Changing them during playback will immediately update playback state.
onTimeUpdate fires approximately 4 times per second during playback (not every frame — this is for efficiency).
backgroundVideo and hoverVideo props are only valid on Box components (not on Video components).
Hover videos require pointer interaction to trigger — they will not play on touch-only devices without hover capability.
Multiple videos can play simultaneously (each with its own mpv instance), but be mindful of GPU/memory limits.

metadata.title: VideoPlayer
metadata.description: Video playback component with built-in Lua-native controls for play/pause, seeking, volume, and fullscreen

sections.overview: VideoPlayer wraps the Video primitive and adds interactive playback controls rendered entirely in Lua. On Love2D it displays play/pause buttons, a draggable seek bar, volume slider, time display, loop toggle, and fullscreen mode. Controls auto-hide after 3 seconds of inactivity and reappear on mouse movement or interaction. On web it falls back to a native HTML5 video element with browser controls. Uses libmpv for hardware-accelerated video decoding and supports local files and HTTP streams, including M3U8/HLS URLs.

examples.title: Basic usage with default controls
Controlled playback with event handling
M3U8/HLS stream playback
Looping background video with no controls

platformNotes.Love2D:
- Renders as a single Lua-native host element. All controls (play/pause button, seek bar, volume slider, time display, loop toggle, fullscreen button) are drawn by Lua's painter, avoiding position:absolute layout issues
- Controls overlay appears on mouse movement and auto-hides after 3 seconds of inactivity
- Play/pause button: Click to toggle playback, or press Space when focused
- Seek bar: Click/drag to scrub, or use Left/Right arrow keys (5-second steps) when focused
- Volume slider: Click/drag to adjust volume (0-1 range), or use Up/Down arrow keys (0.1 steps) when focused
- Mute button: Click to toggle mute
- Loop button: Click to toggle loop mode (active state shown with colored highlight)
- Fullscreen button: Click to toggle fullscreen mode (F11 also works). Fullscreen overrides layout-computed dimensions
- Controls overlay includes gradient background from transparent (top) to semi-black (bottom) for readability
- Seek bar shows elapsed fill and a draggable knob
- Time display shows "current / duration" format (e.g., "1:23 / 5:47")
- Uses libmpv backend for hardware-accelerated video decoding
- MP4, MKV, WebM, OGV, and other mpv-supported formats load directly (no Theora transcode step)
- M3U8/HLS URLs stream directly through mpv
- Video state stored in node._vp (survives re-renders)
- Interacts with videos.lua module for playback control
- Uses measure.lua for font rendering in time display
platformNotes.Web:
- Falls back to HTML5 <video> element with native browser controls
- Controls prop enables/disables browser's built-in UI
- Playback, volume, seeking handled by browser
- Supports any format the browser supports (mp4, webm, ogv, etc.)
- M3U8/HLS support depends on browser (Safari native, others may need hls.js)
- Events normalized to match Love2D API (onTimeUpdate receives { currentTime, duration })

criticalRules: **Always specify explicit width and height** — VideoPlayer has no intrinsic dimensions. Use `w` and `h` props or `style={{ width, height }}`.
**Any mpv-supported format works directly on Love2D** — Use whichever source format matches your pipeline (mp4/webm/mkv/ogv, etc.).
**Don't nest VideoPlayer in auto-sized containers** — It needs explicit dimensions. Put it in a Box with `width: '100%'` or fixed dimensions.
**onTimeUpdate fires frequently** — Throttle expensive operations in the handler (e.g., setState only when seconds change, not on every frame).
**M3U8 streams require network availability** — They are streamed by mpv at runtime. Always handle onError for failed streams.

**Controls are Lua-owned on Love2D** — Don't try to implement custom controls using React components overlaid with position:absolute — that fights the layout engine. Use controls={false} and implement your own Lua-side controls module if needed, or use the bare Video primitive.
**Fullscreen mode is Lua-managed** — When fullscreen, VideoPlayer ignores layout-computed dimensions and fills the entire window. Pressing Esc or clicking the fullscreen button exits.

**Loop and muted state can diverge from props** — User interactions (clicking loop button, mute button) update local state. If you need strict control, hide the controls overlay with controls={false}.

**Don't use percentage dimensions without an explicit parent size** — `w="100%"` requires the parent Box to have a known width. Root containers need `width: '100%', height: '100%'` to fill the viewport.
seeAlso: **Video** — Bare video primitive without controls. Use this for custom UI or when you want full programmatic control.
**Image** — Static image display. Similar sizing/styling props.
**Box** — Container for layout. Use to position VideoPlayer in your UI.
**Modal** — Can wrap VideoPlayer for lightbox-style video display.
**TextEditor** — Used in Example 3 for URL input. Good pattern for loading user-specified videos/streams.

metadata.title: AI Hooks
metadata.description: Hooks for conversational AI, streaming completions, model discovery, API key management, browser control, and MCP server integration.

sections.overview:
The @reactjit/ai package brings LLM integration into ReactJIT apps as composable hooks. It supports OpenAI-compatible APIs and Anthropic natively, handles token streaming over SSE, and runs full agentic tool execution loops without leaving React. Provider configuration is shared via context so API keys and model selection are declared once at the app root and inherited by every hook beneath.

The package is organized into four capability layers:

- Chat and completion — useChat, useCompletion - Provider utilities — useModels, useAIConfig, AIProvider - Key storage — useAPIKeys - Tool integrations — useBrowser, createBrowserTools, useMCPServer

All hooks handle streaming, loading state, and errors uniformly. Tool calls in useChat run concurrently and loop back to the model automatically — the agent loop is built in.
sections.api:
--- AIProvider ---

Wrap your app (or a subtree) to set default AI configuration for all hooks below it. Any hook option passed directly to useChat / useCompletion overrides the context value.

  import { AIProvider } from '@reactjit/ai';

  <AIProvider config={AIConfig}>
    {children}
  </AIProvider>

AIConfig fields:
  provider      AIProviderType   'openai' | 'anthropic' | 'custom'
  model         string           Model ID (e.g. 'gpt-4o', 'claude-opus-4-6')
  apiKey        string?          API key for the provider
  baseURL       string?          Override the API base URL (useful for local models)
  temperature   number?          Sampling temperature (0–2)
  maxTokens     number?          Max tokens in the response
  systemPrompt  string?          System prompt injected into every conversation
  proxy         string?          SOCKS5 or HTTP proxy URL

--- useAIConfig ---

Read the current AI configuration from context. Returns null when no AIProvider ancestor is present; hooks handle this gracefully.

  import { useAIConfig } from '@reactjit/ai';

  const config: AIConfig | null = useAIConfig();

Parameters: none
Returns: AIConfig | null

--- useChat ---

Conversational AI with streaming, message history, and a built-in agentic tool loop. Supports both OpenAI and Anthropic. Streams each token into the live message list as it arrives. When the model returns tool calls, executes them concurrently and feeds results back for the next round — automatically, up to maxToolRounds.

  import { useChat } from '@reactjit/ai';

  const result: ChatResult = useChat(options?: ChatOptions);

ChatOptions fields (all optional — values override the AIProvider context):
  provider        AIProviderType          Provider to use
  model           string                  Model ID
  apiKey          string                  API key
  baseURL         string                  Override base URL
  temperature     number                  Sampling temperature
  maxTokens       number                  Max response tokens
  systemPrompt    string                  System-level instruction
  proxy           string                  SOCKS5/HTTP proxy
  initialMessages Message[]              Pre-load conversation history
  tools           ToolDefinition[]        Tool definitions the model may call
  maxToolRounds   number                  Max agentic loop rounds (default: 10)
  onChunk         (chunk: string) => void Called for each streamed token
  onToolCall      (call: ToolCall) => void Called when the model invokes a tool
  onError         (error: Error) => void  Called on any error

Returns: ChatResult
  messages    Message[]                                  Full conversation history including tool calls and results
  send        (content: string) => Promise<void>         Send a user message and stream the response
  isLoading   boolean                                    True while any round (LLM or tools) is in flight
  isStreaming  boolean                                   True while tokens are arriving from the model
  stop        () => void                                 Abort the current stream and tool loop
  error       Error | null                               Last error, or null
  setMessages (msgs: Message[] | ((prev: Message[]) => Message[])) => void   Direct message history manipulation

Message fields:
  role         MessageRole   'system' | 'user' | 'assistant' | 'tool'
  content      string | ContentBlock[]
  toolCalls    ToolCall[]?   Tool invocations on assistant messages
  toolCallId   string?       On tool result messages, the matching call ID
  name         string?       Tool name on result messages

ToolCall fields:
  id         string   Unique call identifier
  name       string   Tool name
  arguments  string   JSON-encoded argument string

ToolDefinition fields:
  name         string                          Tool name the model uses
  description  string                          Natural language description
  parameters   Record<string, any>             JSON Schema for the arguments
  execute      (args: any) => Promise<any>     Function called when the model invokes the tool

--- useCompletion ---

Single-shot text completion with streaming. Does not maintain conversation history — each call to complete() is independent. Ideal for summaries, transformations, and one-off generation tasks.

  import { useCompletion } from '@reactjit/ai';

  const result: CompletionResult = useCompletion(options?: CompletionOptions);

CompletionOptions fields (all optional — override AIProvider context):
  provider      AIProviderType
  model         string
  apiKey        string
  baseURL       string
  temperature   number
  maxTokens     number
  systemPrompt  string
  proxy         string
  onChunk       (chunk: string) => void
  onError       (error: Error) => void

Returns: CompletionResult
  completion   string                               Full accumulated text so far (updates during streaming)
  complete     (prompt: string) => Promise<string>  Run a completion, resolves with the full text
  isLoading    boolean
  isStreaming  boolean
  stop         () => void
  error        Error | null

--- useModels ---

Fetch the list of available models from a provider. For OpenAI-compatible endpoints, calls GET /v1/models. For Anthropic, returns a static curated list (Anthropic has no models endpoint). Fetches automatically on mount and whenever provider/baseURL/apiKey changes.

  import { useModels } from '@reactjit/ai';

  const result: ModelsResult = useModels(options?: Partial<AIConfig>);

Parameters:
  options  Partial<AIConfig>  Same fields as AIConfig — override context values to query a specific provider

Returns: ModelsResult
  models   ModelInfo[]   Sorted list of available models
  loading  boolean
  error    Error | null
  refetch  () => void    Manually trigger a fresh fetch

ModelInfo fields:
  id        string           Model identifier string (e.g. 'gpt-4o')
  name      string           Display name (equals id for OpenAI-compat)
  provider  AIProviderType

--- useAPIKeys ---

Manage stored API keys. Persists keys across sessions via @reactjit/storage when a StorageProvider ancestor is present; falls back to in-memory storage without one. Keys are stored in a collection named 'ai_keys'.

  import { useAPIKeys } from '@reactjit/ai';

  const result: APIKeysResult = useAPIKeys();

Parameters: none

Returns: APIKeysResult
  keys       APIKeyRecord[]                                            All stored key records
  setKey     (record: Omit<APIKeyRecord, 'id'> & { id?: string }) => Promise<string>   Upsert a key record, returns the assigned ID
  deleteKey  (id: string) => Promise<void>                            Delete a key by ID
  getKey     (provider: AIProviderType) => APIKeyRecord | undefined   Lookup the first key for a provider
  loading    boolean                                                   True while loading keys from storage on mount

APIKeyRecord fields:
  id        string           Auto-generated or caller-supplied identifier
  provider  AIProviderType   Provider this key belongs to
  apiKey    string           The raw API key string
  label     string?          Human-readable label (e.g. 'Work account')
  baseURL   string?          Custom base URL associated with this key
  models    string[]?        Specific models this key has access to

--- useBrowser ---

Control a stealth browse session (anti-fingerprint Firefox) from React components. The browser runs as a separate process on the host machine, listening on TCP port 7331. Commands route through Lua's browse.lua worker thread via the QuickJS bridge. Returns loading and error state so you can reflect browser activity in the UI.

  import { useBrowser } from '@reactjit/ai';

  const browser: BrowseResult = useBrowser(options?: BrowseOptions);

BrowseOptions fields:
  host  string   Browse session host (default: '127.0.0.1')
  port  number   Browse session TCP port (default: 7331)

Returns: BrowseResult
  navigate        (url: string) => Promise<PageContent>                            Go to a URL
  click           (selector: string) => Promise<PageContent>                       Click an element by CSS selector
  typeText        (selector: string, text: string) => Promise<PageContent>         Type into an element (does NOT submit)
  extractContent  () => Promise<PageContent>                                       Re-extract current page content
  screenshot      (path?: string) => Promise<string>                               Take a screenshot (returns base64 PNG or saves to path)
  back            () => Promise<PageContent>                                       Go back in browser history
  forward         () => Promise<PageContent>                                       Go forward in browser history
  refresh         () => Promise<PageContent>                                       Reload the current page
  executeJs       (script: string) => Promise<any>                                 Run JavaScript in the page context
  listTabs        () => Promise<{ index: number; title: string; url: string }[]>   List all open tabs
  useTab          (index: number) => Promise<PageContent>                          Switch to a tab by index
  openTab         (url: string) => Promise<void>                                   Open a new background tab
  ping            () => Promise<boolean>                                           Check if the browse session is reachable
  raw             (cmd: Record<string, any>) => Promise<any>                       Send an arbitrary command
  loading         boolean                                                           True while a command is in flight
  error           Error | null

PageContent fields:
  url    string
  title  string
  text   string
  links  { text: string; href: string }[]
  forms  { id: string; action: string; inputs: any[] }[]

--- createBrowserTools ---

Build a ToolDefinition[] array that gives an AI agent full control over the browser. Pass the result directly into useChat's tools option. The agent can then navigate, click, type, take screenshots, and manage tabs autonomously using natural language instructions.

  import { createBrowserTools } from '@reactjit/ai';

  const tools: ToolDefinition[] = createBrowserTools(options?: BrowseOptions);

Parameters:
  options  BrowseOptions  Same host/port options as useBrowser

Returns: ToolDefinition[]

Tools exposed to the model:
  browser_navigate     Go to a URL
  browser_click        Click an element by CSS selector
  browser_type         Type text into an input field
  browser_extract      Re-extract current page content (for AJAX updates)
  browser_back         Go back in history
  browser_tabs         List open tabs
  browser_use_tab      Switch to a tab by index
  browser_open_tab     Open a new background tab
  browser_execute_js   Run JavaScript in page context
  browser_screenshot   Take a screenshot

Page content returned to the model is automatically truncated to 8000 characters and 50 links to avoid flooding the context window.

--- useMCPServer ---

Connect to an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server and expose its tools as ToolDefinitions ready to pass into useChat. Handles the full lifecycle: connect, initialize handshake, tool discovery, permission filtering, and optional confirmation gates. Reconnects automatically when transport parameters change.

  import { useMCPServer } from '@reactjit/ai';

  const result: MCPServerResult = useMCPServer(config: MCPServerConfig);

MCPServerConfig fields:
  name         string               Human-readable name for the server (used in error messages)
  transport    MCPTransportType     'stdio' | 'sse' | 'streamable-http'
  command      string?              (stdio) Command to spawn (e.g. 'npx')
  args         string[]?            (stdio) Arguments to the command
  env          Record<string,string>? (stdio) Extra environment variables
  url          string?              (sse / streamable-http) Server endpoint URL
  headers      Record<string,string>? (sse / streamable-http) Extra request headers
  timeout      number?              Request timeout in ms (default: 30000)
  permissions  MCPPermissionsConfig?  Allowlist of tools to expose (required — no permissions = no tools)
  onConfirm    (toolName: string, args: any) => Promise<boolean>?  Gate for tools with confirm: true

MCPPermissionsConfig shape:
  tools   Record<string, MCPToolPermission>   Tool name → permission entry
  lastDiscovered   string?                    ISO timestamp of last tool discovery
  tokenBudget      { totalIfAllEnabled: number; note: string }?

MCPToolPermission fields:
  enabled        boolean   Whether to expose this tool to the model
  confirm        boolean   Whether to call onConfirm before executing
  description    string?   Override the tool description shown to the model
  inputSchema    Record<string, any>?   Override the tool's input schema
  tokenEstimate  number?   Pre-computed token cost of this tool's definition

Returns: MCPServerResult
  status          'connecting' | 'ready' | 'error'   Current connection state
  tools           ToolDefinition[]                    Enabled tools, ready for useChat
  availableTools  string[]                            All tool names discovered from the server (regardless of permissions)
  error           Error | null
  disconnect      () => void                          Manually close the connection

--- MCPClient ---

Low-level MCP client class. Used by useMCPServer internally and available for imperative use cases such as the CLI linter, build-time tool discovery, or custom React effects.

  import { MCPClient } from '@reactjit/ai';

  const client = new MCPClient(config: MCPServerConfig);

Methods:
  connect()                                       Connect and complete the initialize handshake
  listTools(): Promise<MCPTool[]>                 Discover all tools from the server
  callTool(name, args): Promise<MCPToolCallResult>  Call a named tool
  close()                                         Close the connection
  getServerInfo()                                 Return the server name and version from the handshake

Properties:
  status  MCPClientStatus   'disconnected' | 'connecting' | 'ready' | 'error'
  error   Error | null

MCPTool fields:
  name         string
  description  string
  inputSchema  Record<string, any>   JSON Schema for arguments

--- estimateToolTokens / estimateToolBudget ---

Estimate the context window cost of tool definitions before passing them to a model. Useful for deciding which MCP tools to enable when working with tight context budgets.

  import { estimateToolTokens, estimateToolBudget } from '@reactjit/ai';

  const tokens: number = estimateToolTokens(tool: MCPTool);
  const budget: { total: number; note: string } = estimateToolBudget(tools: MCPTool[]);

estimateToolTokens:
  Parameters: tool  MCPTool
  Returns: number — estimated token count for that tool's definition

estimateToolBudget:
  Parameters: tools  MCPTool[]
  Returns: { total: number; note: string } — total tokens and a human-readable percentage of a 128K context window
seeAlso: useFetch — plain HTTP requests without the LLM layer
useWebSocket — raw WebSocket connections for custom streaming
useLoveRPC — low-level Lua bridge used internally by the browse integration
StorageProvider — required ancestor for useAPIKeys persistence across sessions

metadata.title: Audio Hooks
metadata.description: Hooks for building modular synthesizers, samplers, drum machines, sequencers, and MIDI controllers in Love2D.

sections.overview: The @reactjit/audio package exposes a modular synthesis system as React hooks. Audio modules (oscillators, filters, envelopes, mixers, samplers, clocks, sequencers) are wired together into a signal graph called the rack. State flows from the Lua audio engine into React via bridge events; commands flow back via RPC. MIDI input is also surfaced through this package, including device enumeration, CC-to-parameter mapping, and a learn mode for real-time controller assignment. The package also provides sample file loading, microphone recording, a sample-accurate BPM clock, and a step sequencer for building drum machines and other rhythmic applications.
sections.api:
--- useAudioInit ---

Initialize the audio engine. Call this once at the root of your app before using any other audio hooks. Returns true when the engine is ready. Resolves even if audio hardware is unavailable, so the UI always renders.

  import { useAudioInit } from '@reactjit/audio';

  const ready: boolean = useAudioInit();

Parameters: none
Returns: boolean — true once the engine has initialized (or failed gracefully)

--- useRack ---

Access the full audio rack: the list of active modules, the connections between them, and operations to mutate the graph.

  import { useRack } from '@reactjit/audio';

  const rack: UseRackResult = useRack();

Parameters: none

Returns: UseRackResult
  modules       ModuleState[]   All modules currently in the rack
  connections   Connection[]    All active port connections
  addModule     (type: string, id: string, params?: Record<string, any>) => Promise<any>
  removeModule  (id: string) => Promise<any>
  connect       (fromId: string, fromPort: string, toId: string, toPort: string) => Promise<any>
  disconnect    (fromId: string, fromPort: string, toId: string, toPort: string) => Promise<any>

ModuleState fields:
  id           string
  type         string
  params       Record<string, any>
  ports        Record<string, { type: PortType; direction: PortDirection }>
  activeNotes  Record<string, { note: number; envelope: number }> | undefined

Connection fields:
  fromId    string
  fromPort  string
  toId      string
  toPort    string
  type      PortType   ('audio' | 'control' | 'midi')

--- useModule ---

Access and control a single module by its ID. Subscribes to rack state updates and surfaces only the named module's params and ports.

  import { useModule } from '@reactjit/audio';

  const mod: UseModuleResult = useModule(moduleId: string);

Parameters:
  moduleId  string  The ID assigned to the module when it was added

Returns: UseModuleResult
  id           string
  type         string
  params       Record<string, any>
  ports        Record<string, { type: PortType; direction: PortDirection }>
  activeNotes  Record<string, { note: number; envelope: number }> | undefined
  setParam     (name: string, value: any) => Promise<any>

--- useParam ---

Read and write a single parameter on a module. Provides an optimistic update: the local value is set immediately while the RPC call propagates to Lua.

  import { useParam } from '@reactjit/audio';

  const [value, setParam] = useParam(moduleId: string, paramName: string);

Parameters:
  moduleId   string  Module to target
  paramName  string  Parameter name on that module

Returns: [any, (value: any) => Promise<any>]
  [0]  Current parameter value (null until first rack state event)
  [1]  Setter — sends RPC and applies optimistic update

--- useMIDI ---

Access connected MIDI devices, current CC mappings, learn mode state, and operations to add or remove mappings.

  import { useMIDI } from '@reactjit/audio';

  const midi: UseMIDIResult = useMIDI();

Parameters: none

Returns: UseMIDIResult
  available  boolean          Whether a MIDI subsystem is available
  devices    MIDIDevice[]     Connected devices
  mappings   MIDIMapping[]    Active CC-to-parameter mappings
  learning   { moduleId: string; param: string } | null   Active learn target, or null
  learn      (moduleId: string, param: string) => Promise<any>   Arm learn mode
  map        (moduleId: string, param: string, channel: number, cc: number) => Promise<any>
  unmap      (moduleId: string, param: string) => Promise<any>

MIDIDevice fields:
  id         string
  name       string
  connected  boolean

MIDIMapping fields:
  channel   number
  cc        number
  moduleId  string
  param     string

--- useMIDINote ---

Subscribe to raw MIDI note on/off events from any connected device. The handler is stable — reassigning it between renders does not re-subscribe.

  import { useMIDINote } from '@reactjit/audio';

  useMIDINote(handler: (event: MIDINoteEvent) => void);

Parameters:
  handler  (event: MIDINoteEvent) => void

Returns: void

MIDINoteEvent fields:
  note      number   MIDI note number (0–127)
  velocity  number   Velocity (0–127; 0 on note-off)
  on        boolean  true = note on, false = note off
  channel   number   MIDI channel (1–16)
  device    string   Device ID

--- useMIDICC ---

Subscribe to raw MIDI CC (continuous controller) events from any connected device.

  import { useMIDICC } from '@reactjit/audio';

  useMIDICC(handler: (event: MIDICCEvent) => void);

Parameters:
  handler  (event: MIDICCEvent) => void

Returns: void

MIDICCEvent fields:
  cc       number   CC number (0–127)
  value    number   CC value (0–127)
  channel  number   MIDI channel (1–16)
  device   string   Device ID

--- useClock ---

Access a clock module's beat position and transport controls. The clock runs sample-accurate in the Lua audio engine. Position updates arrive via the 30fps state push.

  import { useClock } from '@reactjit/audio';

  const clock: UseClockResult = useClock(moduleId: string);

Parameters:
  moduleId  string  The clock module's ID

Returns: UseClockResult
  beat         number   Current beat within the bar (0–3)
  bar          number   Current bar number
  step         number   Current step (depends on division)
  phase        number   Fractional position within current step (0–1)
  running      boolean  Whether the clock is ticking
  bpm          number   Current tempo
  start        () => Promise<any>   Start the clock
  stop         () => Promise<any>   Stop the clock
  setBpm       (bpm: number) => Promise<any>   Set tempo
  setDivision  (division: string) => Promise<any>   Set division ("1/4", "1/8", "1/16", "1/32")
  setSwing     (swing: number) => Promise<any>   Set swing amount (0–1)

--- useClockEvent ---

Subscribe to clock tick events. Fires on each tick at the audio engine's rate. The handler is stable across re-renders.

  import { useClockEvent } from '@reactjit/audio';

  useClockEvent(handler: (event: ClockTickEvent) => void);

Parameters:
  handler  (event: ClockTickEvent) => void

Returns: void

ClockTickEvent fields:
  beat  number  Beat within bar
  bar   number  Bar number
  step  number  Step number
  bpm   number  Current tempo

--- useSampler ---

Manage a sampler module's slots: load audio files, clear slots, trigger playback. Samples are loaded from the Love2D filesystem. MIDI note 36 = slot 1, 37 = slot 2, etc. (GM drum map starting at C2).

  import { useSampler } from '@reactjit/audio';

  const sampler: UseSamplerResult = useSampler(moduleId: string);

Parameters:
  moduleId  string  The sampler module's ID

Returns: UseSamplerResult
  slots        Record<number, SampleSlot | null>  Loaded sample info per slot (1–16)
  voices       SamplerVoice[]                     Currently playing voices
  loadSample   (slot: number, path: string, mode?: 'oneshot' | 'loop') => Promise<any>
  clearSample  (slot: number) => Promise<any>
  trigger      (slot: number, velocity?: number) => Promise<any>

SampleSlot fields:
  name        string   Filename of the loaded sample
  duration    number   Duration in seconds
  sampleRate  number   Original sample rate
  mode        string   "oneshot" or "loop"

SamplerVoice fields:
  slot      number  Which slot is playing
  position  number  Current playback position in seconds
  duration  number  Total duration of the sample

--- useRecorder ---

Record audio from a microphone or input device into a sampler slot. Uses Love2D's RecordingDevice API. Call listDevices() to enumerate available inputs, startRecording() to begin capture, and stopRecording() to finalize the sample into its target slot.

  import { useRecorder } from '@reactjit/audio';

  const recorder: UseRecorderResult = useRecorder();

Parameters: none

Returns: UseRecorderResult
  devices         AudioRecordingDevice[]  Available recording devices
  recording       RecordingState          Current recording status
  listDevices     () => Promise<any>      Enumerate available devices
  startRecording  (moduleId: string, slot: number, deviceIndex?: number) => Promise<any>
  stopRecording   () => Promise<any>

AudioRecordingDevice fields:
  index  number  Device index (1-based)
  name   string  Device name

RecordingState fields:
  active    boolean        Whether recording is in progress
  moduleId  string | null  Target sampler module
  slot      number | null  Target slot
  device    string | null  Recording device name
  duration  number         Elapsed recording time in seconds

--- useSequencer ---

Control a step sequencer module's pattern and track assignments. The sequencer advances on rising edges from a connected clock module's gate output. Each track targets a different module for note triggering.

  import { useSequencer } from '@reactjit/audio';

  const seq: UseSequencerResult = useSequencer(moduleId: string);

Parameters:
  moduleId  string  The sequencer module's ID

Returns: UseSequencerResult
  pattern         Record<string, Record<string, StepData>>  Pattern grid [track][step]
  currentStep     number                                     Current step position
  trackTargets    Record<string, string>                     Track → target module ID
  setStep         (track: number, step: number, active: boolean, note?: number, velocity?: number) => Promise<any>
  setTrackTarget  (track: number, targetModuleId: string) => Promise<any>
  clearPattern    () => Promise<any>

StepData fields:
  active    boolean  Whether this step fires
  note      number   MIDI note number (default 36)
  velocity  number   Velocity (default 100)
seeAlso: useLoveRPC — low-level RPC bridge used internally by all audio hooks
useLoveEvent — low-level event subscription used internally by all audio hooks

metadata.title: Crypto Hooks
metadata.description: Audited cryptographic primitives — hashing, symmetric encryption, digital signatures, key exchange, and secure token generation — built on @noble/hashes, @noble/ciphers, and @noble/curves.

sections.overview: The @reactjit/crypto package exposes pure-JavaScript cryptographic primitives that work across every ReactJIT target, including QuickJS inside Love2D. All implementations come from the @noble suite — a set of audited, dependency-free libraries that are widely used in production (millions of weekly downloads, no native code). The package is organized into four functional groups: hashing (sha256, sha512, BLAKE2b/2s/3, HMAC), symmetric encryption (AES-256-GCM, ChaCha20-Poly1305 with scrypt KDF), asymmetric signatures/key exchange (Ed25519, X25519), and token generation. Encoding utilities (hex, base64) are also re-exported. None of these are React hooks — they are synchronous or Promise-returning functions you call directly from hooks, effects, or event handlers.
sections.api:
--- sha256 ---

Compute a SHA-256 digest.

  import { sha256 } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const result: HashResult = sha256(input: string | Uint8Array);

Parameters:
  input  string | Uint8Array   Data to hash (strings are UTF-8 encoded)

Returns: HashResult
  hex    string       Lowercase hex digest (64 chars)
  base64 string       Base64-encoded digest
  bytes  Uint8Array   Raw 32-byte digest

--- sha512 ---

Compute a SHA-512 digest.

  import { sha512 } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const result: HashResult = sha512(input: string | Uint8Array);

Parameters:
  input  string | Uint8Array

Returns: HashResult (128-char hex, 64 raw bytes)

--- hash_blake2b ---

BLAKE2b with configurable output length. Default: 32 bytes.

  import { hash_blake2b } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const result: HashResult = hash_blake2b(input: string | Uint8Array, bytes?: number);

Parameters:
  input  string | Uint8Array
  bytes  number   Output length in bytes. Default: 32. Range: 1–64.

Returns: HashResult

--- hash_blake2s ---

BLAKE2s (always 32 bytes). Faster than BLAKE2b on 32-bit platforms.

  import { hash_blake2s } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const result: HashResult = hash_blake2s(input: string | Uint8Array);

Parameters:
  input  string | Uint8Array

Returns: HashResult

--- hash_blake3 ---

BLAKE3 with configurable output length. Default: 32 bytes. Fastest of the three BLAKE variants.

  import { hash_blake3 } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const result: HashResult = hash_blake3(input: string | Uint8Array, bytes?: number);

Parameters:
  input  string | Uint8Array
  bytes  number   Output length in bytes. Default: 32.

Returns: HashResult

--- hmacSHA256 ---

HMAC-SHA256. Use for message authentication, API request signing, and webhook signature generation.

  import { hmacSHA256 } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const result: HashResult = hmacSHA256(key: string | Uint8Array, message: string | Uint8Array);

Parameters:
  key      string | Uint8Array   Shared secret
  message  string | Uint8Array   Message to authenticate

Returns: HashResult (64-char hex)

--- hmacSHA512 ---

HMAC-SHA512. Same semantics as hmacSHA256 with a 128-char hex output.

  import { hmacSHA512 } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const result: HashResult = hmacSHA512(key: string | Uint8Array, message: string | Uint8Array);

Returns: HashResult

--- timingSafeEqual ---

Compare two byte sequences in constant time. Safe for comparing secrets — the comparison time does not depend on where the first difference appears.

  import { timingSafeEqual } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const equal: boolean = timingSafeEqual(a: string | Uint8Array, b: string | Uint8Array);

Parameters:
  a  string | Uint8Array
  b  string | Uint8Array

Returns: boolean — false immediately (with length leak) if lengths differ; otherwise constant-time value comparison.

--- encrypt ---

Password-based authenticated encryption using scrypt KDF + AES-256-GCM (default) or ChaCha20-Poly1305. Returns a JSON-serializable EncryptedData envelope that bundles the algorithm, ciphertext, nonce, salt, and KDF parameters together so the receiver does not need to remember them separately.

  import { encrypt } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const envelope: EncryptedData = encrypt(plaintext: string, password: string, options?: EncryptOptions);

Parameters:
  plaintext  string          Data to encrypt
  password   string          User-provided password (derived to a 256-bit key via scrypt)
  options    EncryptOptions  Optional configuration

EncryptOptions fields:
  algorithm  'aes-256-gcm' | 'chacha20-poly1305' | 'xchacha20-poly1305'
             Default: 'aes-256-gcm'. Choose chacha20-poly1305 on platforms without hardware AES acceleration.

Returns: EncryptedData
  algorithm   'aes-256-gcm' | 'chacha20-poly1305' | 'xchacha20-poly1305'
  ciphertext  string   Base64-encoded ciphertext (includes GCM/Poly1305 authentication tag)
  nonce       string   Base64-encoded nonce/IV
  salt        string   Base64-encoded 32-byte salt
  kdf         'scrypt'
  kdfParams   { N, r, p }   scrypt cost parameters (N=131072, r=8, p=1 by default)

--- decrypt ---

Decrypt an EncryptedData envelope produced by encrypt. Reads KDF parameters from the envelope, re-derives the key, and authenticates + decrypts.

  import { decrypt } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const plaintext: string = decrypt(data: EncryptedData, password: string);

Parameters:
  data      EncryptedData   Envelope from encrypt (or stored JSON)
  password  string          The same password used to encrypt

Returns: string — decrypted plaintext
Throws on wrong password (authentication tag mismatch) or malformed input.

--- encryptRaw ---

Encrypt raw bytes with a caller-managed 32-byte key. No KDF step. Use this when you already have a key from a Diffie-Hellman exchange or another key derivation flow.

  import { encryptRaw } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const { ciphertext, nonce } = encryptRaw(
    plaintext: Uint8Array,
    key: Uint8Array,
    algo?: 'aes-256-gcm' | 'chacha20-poly1305' | 'xchacha20-poly1305',
  );

Parameters:
  plaintext  Uint8Array   Data to encrypt
  key        Uint8Array   32-byte key
  algo       string       Encryption algorithm. Default: 'aes-256-gcm'

Returns: { ciphertext: Uint8Array, nonce: Uint8Array }

--- decryptRaw ---

Decrypt raw bytes with a caller-managed key and nonce.

  import { decryptRaw } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const plaintext: Uint8Array = decryptRaw(
    ciphertext: Uint8Array,
    key: Uint8Array,
    nonce: Uint8Array,
    algo?: 'aes-256-gcm' | 'chacha20-poly1305' | 'xchacha20-poly1305',
  );

Parameters:
  ciphertext  Uint8Array
  key         Uint8Array   32-byte key
  nonce       Uint8Array   Nonce used during encryption
  algo        string       Must match the algorithm used during encryption. Default: 'aes-256-gcm'

Returns: Uint8Array — decrypted bytes
Throws on authentication failure.

--- generateSigningKeys ---

Generate an Ed25519 key pair for digital signatures.

  import { generateSigningKeys } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const keys: KeyPair = generateSigningKeys();

Parameters: none

Returns: KeyPair
  publicKey   string   Hex-encoded Ed25519 public key (64 chars)
  privateKey  string   Hex-encoded Ed25519 private key (64 chars)
  curve       'ed25519'

--- sign ---

Sign a message string with an Ed25519 private key.

  import { sign } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const signed: SignedMessage = sign(privateKeyHex: string, message: string);

Parameters:
  privateKeyHex  string   Hex-encoded private key from generateSigningKeys
  message        string   Message to sign

Returns: SignedMessage
  message    string   The original message
  signature  string   Hex-encoded Ed25519 signature (128 chars)
  publicKey  string   Hex-encoded public key derived from the private key
  algorithm  'ed25519'

--- verify ---

Verify a SignedMessage produced by sign.

  import { verify } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const valid: boolean = verify(signed: SignedMessage);

Parameters:
  signed  SignedMessage   Envelope from sign (message + signature + publicKey)

Returns: boolean

--- verifyDetached ---

Verify a detached signature (when you have the three components separately rather than a SignedMessage envelope).

  import { verifyDetached } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const valid: boolean = verifyDetached(
    message: string,
    signatureHex: string,
    publicKeyHex: string,
  );

Parameters:
  message       string   Original message
  signatureHex  string   Hex-encoded signature
  publicKeyHex  string   Hex-encoded public key

Returns: boolean

--- generateDHKeys ---

Generate an X25519 key pair for Diffie-Hellman key exchange.

  import { generateDHKeys } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const keys: KeyPair = generateDHKeys();

Parameters: none

Returns: KeyPair
  publicKey   string   Hex-encoded X25519 public key (64 chars)
  privateKey  string   Hex-encoded X25519 private key (64 chars)
  curve       'x25519'

--- diffieHellman ---

Compute a shared secret from your X25519 private key and the other party's public key. Both sides derive the same secret without transmitting it.

  import { diffieHellman } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const sharedSecret: string = diffieHellman(privateKeyHex: string, publicKeyHex: string);

Parameters:
  privateKeyHex  string   Your hex-encoded X25519 private key
  publicKeyHex   string   The other party's hex-encoded X25519 public key

Returns: string — hex-encoded 32-byte shared secret

--- randomBytes ---

Generate cryptographically random bytes. Re-exported from @noble/hashes.

  import { randomBytes } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const bytes: Uint8Array = randomBytes(length: number);

Parameters:
  length  number   Number of bytes

Returns: Uint8Array

--- randomToken ---

Generate a cryptographically random hex string. Use for API keys, session IDs, and CSRF tokens.

  import { randomToken } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const token: string = randomToken(bytes?: number);

Parameters:
  bytes  number   Byte length. Default: 32 (produces a 64-char hex string)

Returns: string — lowercase hex (length = bytes * 2)

--- randomBase64 ---

Generate a cryptographically random base64 string.

  import { randomBase64 } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const token: string = randomBase64(bytes?: number);

Parameters:
  bytes  number   Byte length. Default: 32 (produces ~44 base64 chars)

Returns: string

--- randomId ---

Generate a cryptographically random alphanumeric string. URL-safe with no special characters.

  import { randomId } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  const id: string = randomId(length?: number);

Parameters:
  length  number   Character length of the output. Default: 16

Returns: string — alphanumeric characters only (A-Z, a-z, 0-9)

--- Encoding utilities ---

  import { toHex, fromHex, toBase64, fromBase64 } from '@reactjit/crypto';

  toHex(bytes: Uint8Array): string            — Uint8Array to lowercase hex
  fromHex(hex: string): Uint8Array            — hex string to Uint8Array
  toBase64(bytes: Uint8Array): string         — Uint8Array to base64 string
  fromBase64(base64: string): Uint8Array      — base64 string to Uint8Array
seeAlso: useWebhook — uses hmacSHA256 for incoming webhook signature verification
useStorage — pairs well with encrypt/decrypt for persisting sensitive settings
useFetch — for authenticated API requests where you need to sign request bodies

metadata.title: Hooks
metadata.description: Overview of ReactJIT hooks for Love2D communication, state sharing, and event handling.

sections.overview:
ReactJIT provides a set of React hooks for communicating between the React component tree and the Lua runtime (or any target-specific backend). These hooks consume the bridge interface (IBridge) via React context, so they work identically regardless of the transport layer (Module.FS for web, QuickJS FFI for native Love2D, WebSocket for remote targets).

The hooks cover three patterns: event subscription (useLove, useLoveEvent), RPC calls (useLoveRPC), and shared state (useLoveState). Additional utility hooks provide readiness detection (useLoveReady), fire-and-forget sending (useLoveSend), and DOM overlay positioning (useLoveOverlays, web mode only).
sections.api:
All hooks are imported from '@reactjit/core':

  import {
    useLove,
    useLoveEvent,
    useLoveRPC,
    useLoveState,
    useLoveReady,
    useLoveSend,
    useLoveOverlays,
  } from '@reactjit/core';

All hooks require a BridgeProvider ancestor in the component tree, which is set up
automatically by the framework's entry point.

examples.title: Subscribe to events and send commands
Bidirectional shared state

platformNotes.by target:
- Love2D native: QuickJS FFI bridge (bridge_quickjs.lua)
- Web: Module.FS bridge (in-memory event queue)
- Terminal/grid targets: Bridge over IPC or WebSocket
useLoveOverlays is only meaningful in web mode where React controls the DOM and needs to
position HTML overlays relative to a canvas element.

criticalRules: All hooks require BridgeProvider context -- they will throw if used outside it
useLoveEvent uses a ref for the handler to avoid re-subscribing on handler changes
useLoveState updates both local React state and Lua-side state bidirectionally
useLoveRPC returns an async function -- always await the result
Hook event type strings must match the event types dispatched by the Lua side
seeAlso: uselove.txt -- Event subscription with send function

metadata.title: Media Hooks
metadata.description: Hooks for scanning media libraries, listing archive contents, reading files from inside archives, and deep-indexing directories.

sections.overview: The @reactjit/media package provides two sets of hooks: archive hooks for working with compressed files (RAR, ZIP, 7z, TAR, and others), and media library hooks for scanning directories of media files. The archive hooks delegate to a Lua-side libarchive integration; the scan hooks walk the filesystem. useMediaIndex combines both — it looks inside archives to surface their contents as first-class MediaFile entries alongside regular files.
sections.api:
--- useArchive ---

List all entries inside an archive. Fetches automatically when filepath changes. Provides a refresh function for manual re-fetching.

  import { useArchive } from '@reactjit/media';

  const { entries, loading, error, refresh } = useArchive(filepath: string | null);

Parameters:
  filepath  string | null  Absolute path to the archive file. Pass null to idle.

Returns:
  entries   ArchiveEntry[]  All entries in the archive (files, directories, symlinks)
  loading   boolean
  error     string | null
  refresh   () => Promise<void>   Re-fetch the entry list

ArchiveEntry fields:
  path       string                        Path within the archive
  size       number                        Size in bytes
  type       'file' | 'directory' | 'symlink'
  mtime      number                        Modification timestamp (Unix seconds)
  encrypted  boolean

--- useArchiveInfo ---

Fetch a summary of an archive without listing every entry. Returns counts, total size, and an extension histogram.

  import { useArchiveInfo } from '@reactjit/media';

  const { info, loading, error } = useArchiveInfo(filepath: string | null);

Parameters:
  filepath  string | null

Returns:
  info     ArchiveInfo | null
  loading  boolean
  error    string | null

ArchiveInfo fields:
  totalEntries  number
  totalSize     number                        Total uncompressed bytes
  fileCount     number
  dirCount      number
  extensions    Record<string, number>        Counts per extension, e.g. { mp4: 3, srt: 3 }

--- useArchiveRead ---

Returns an imperative function that reads a single file from inside an archive. Call it on demand rather than on mount. Throws on error.

  import { useArchiveRead } from '@reactjit/media';

  const read = useArchiveRead();
  const { content, size } = await read(filepath, entryPath, maxBytes?);

Parameters (of the returned function):
  filepath   string   Absolute path to the archive
  entryPath  string   Path of the entry within the archive
  maxBytes   number   (optional) Byte limit on the returned content

Returns: Promise<{ content: string; size: number }>
  content  string   File content as a string
  size     number   Full entry size in bytes (may exceed maxBytes if truncated)

Throws: Error with message from Lua if the entry cannot be read.

--- useArchiveSearch ---

Search archive entries by a Lua pattern. Fetches automatically when filepath or pattern changes.

  import { useArchiveSearch } from '@reactjit/media';

  const { matches, loading } = useArchiveSearch(filepath: string | null, pattern: string);

Parameters:
  filepath  string | null  Archive to search. Pass null to idle.
  pattern   string         Lua pattern matched against each entry path (e.g. '%.mp4$')

Returns:
  matches  ArchiveEntry[]
  loading  boolean

--- useMediaLibrary ---

Scan a directory for media files. Returns typed MediaFile entries with source set to 'filesystem'. Does not look inside archives; use useMediaIndex for that.

  import { useMediaLibrary } from '@reactjit/media';

  const { files, stats, loading, error, rescan } = useMediaLibrary(
    directory: string | null,
    options?: MediaLibraryOptions,
  );

Parameters:
  directory  string | null
  options    MediaLibraryOptions (optional)

MediaLibraryOptions fields:
  recursive  boolean      Scan subdirectories (default true)
  maxDepth   number       Maximum recursion depth (default 10)
  autoScan   boolean      Scan on mount (default true)
  filter     MediaType[]  Only return entries of these types

Returns:
  files    MediaFile[]   All scanned files matching the filter
  stats    DirStats      Computed from the returned files (see below)
  loading  boolean
  error    string | null
  rescan   () => Promise<void>

MediaFile fields:
  path          string                        Full filesystem path
  name          string                        Filename only
  size          number                        Bytes
  mtime         number | undefined            Modification timestamp (Unix seconds)
  type          MediaType                     Classified type
  source        'filesystem' | 'archive'
  archivePath   string | undefined            Set when source is 'archive'
  archiveEntry  string | undefined            Set when source is 'archive'

MediaType values:
  'video' | 'audio' | 'image' | 'subtitle' | 'document' | 'archive' | 'metadata' | 'unknown'

DirStats fields:
  total        number
  byType       Partial<Record<MediaType, number>>
  totalSize    number
  largestFile  MediaFile | null

--- useMediaStats ---

Fetch quick directory statistics without loading every file. Lighter than useMediaLibrary when you only need counts and sizes.

  import { useMediaStats } from '@reactjit/media';

  const { stats, loading } = useMediaStats(directory: string | null);

Parameters:
  directory  string | null

Returns:
  stats    DirStats | null
  loading  boolean

--- useMediaIndex ---

Deep-index a directory, scanning both filesystem files and the contents of archives. Archive contents appear as MediaFile entries with source set to 'archive'. This is the archive walker.

  import { useMediaIndex } from '@reactjit/media';

  const { index, loading, error, rescan } = useMediaIndex(
    directory: string | null,
    options?: MediaIndexOptions,
  );

Parameters:
  directory  string | null
  options    MediaIndexOptions (optional)

MediaIndexOptions fields:
  indexArchives    boolean   Look inside archives (default true)
  archivePattern   string    Only index archives whose filename matches this Lua pattern
  filter           MediaType[]   Filter results by type

Returns:
  index    MediaFile[]   Flat list of all discovered files including archive contents
  loading  boolean
  error    string | null
  rescan   () => Promise<void>
seeAlso: useLoveRPC — low-level RPC bridge used internally by all media hooks
classifyFile — exported utility that classifies a filename to a MediaType without an RPC call
formatSize — exported utility that formats a byte count to a human-readable string (KB/MB/GB/TB)

metadata.title: RSS Hooks
metadata.description: Hooks for subscribing to RSS 2.0, Atom, and RSS 1.0 feeds with polling, deduplication, and multi-feed aggregation.

sections.overview: The @reactjit/rss package turns any RSS/Atom feed URL into a reactive data source. It ships a zero-dependency XML parser that runs in QuickJS (Love2D), Node.js, and browsers without requiring DOMParser. The two main hooks — useRSSFeed and useRSSAggregate — handle fetching, polling, deduplication, and limit enforcement. Imperative helpers (fetchFeed, parseFeed) and OPML import/export utilities round out the package for building complete feed readers.
sections.api:
--- useRSSFeed ---
